1 /** 2 * Competition invite claim landing route. 3 * 4 * The athlete clicks the `/compete/$slug/claim/$token` link in the email. 5 * The loader: 6 * 1. resolves the invite via `getInviteByTokenFn` (hashes + looks up), 7 * 2. runs `identityMatch` against the current session, 8 * 3. branches — redirect for sign-in / sign-up, render rejection for 9 * wrong account, render error for expired / declined / revoked, or 10 * render the claim page for the happy path. 11 * 12 * The claim page itself is thin in Phase 2: it confirms the invite and 13 * sends the athlete into the existing registration flow at 14 * `/compete/$slug/register?divisionId=`. Phase 2 sub-arc C extends 15 * `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` to accept `inviteToken` so the paid 16 * registration flips the invite to `accepted_paid`. 17 */ 18 19 import { createFileRoute, Link, redirect } from "@tanstack/react-router" 20 import { AlertCircle, CheckCircle2, LogOut, Ticket, UserX } from "lucide-react" 21 import { useState } from "react" 22 import { Alert, AlertDescription, AlertTitle } from "@/components/ui/alert" 23 import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" 24 import { 25 Card, 26 CardContent, 27 CardDescription, 28 CardHeader, 29 CardTitle, 30 } from "@/components/ui/card" 31 import { 32 type InviteClaimableError, 33 identityMatch, 34 } from "@/server/competition-invites/identity" 35 import { logoutFn } from "@/server-fns/auth-fns" 36 import { getInviteByTokenFn } from "@/server-fns/competition-invite-fns" 37 38 type Branch = 39 | { 40 kind: "claimable" 41 divisionId: string 42 divisionLabel: string 43 championshipName: string 44 } 45 | { 46 kind: "wrong_account" 47 championshipName: string 48 inviteEmail: string 49 accountExistsForInviteEmail: boolean 50 } 51 | { 52 kind: "already_claimed" 53 championshipName: string 54 registrationId: string 55 } 56 | { 57 kind: "over_allocated" 58 championshipName?: string 59 } 60 | { kind: "invalid"; reason: InviteClaimableError; championshipName?: string } 61 62 export const Route = createFileRoute("/compete/$slug/claim/$token")({ 63 component: ClaimPage, 64 staleTime: 0, 65 loader: async ({ params, context, parentMatchPromise }): Promise => { 66 const result = await getInviteByTokenFn({ 67 data: { slug: params.slug, token: params.token }, 68 }) 69 70 if (result.kind === "not_claimable") { 71 // Already-registered athletes shouldn't see a destructive "error" page — 72 // they're not in an error state, they just landed on the wrong page. 73 // Send them to their registration view. Covers both an invite that was 74 // already consumed (status = accepted_paid) and the cross-check against 75 // an active registration created via any lane. 76 if (result.reason === "already_paid") { 77 throw redirect({ 78 to: "/compete/$slug/registered", 79 params: { slug: params.slug }, 80 search: { session_id: undefined, registration_id: undefined }, 81 }) 82 } 83 84 // ADR-0012 Phase 5: source-attributed allocation filled. Render a 85 // soft (info-style) page rather than a destructive error — the 86 // athlete isn't at fault, the spot just isn't available from this 87 // source for this division. 88 if (result.reason === "over_allocated") { 89 return { 90 kind: "over_allocated", 91 championshipName: 92 "championshipName" in result ? result.championshipName : undefined, 93 } 94 } 95 96 // `not_found` happens both when the link is bogus AND when the original 97 // invite has been consumed and the token hash nulled (replay-safe). To 98 // tell them apart, peek at the parent route's `userRegistrations`: if 99 // the signed-in visitor already has an active registration in this 100 // competition, render the friendly "already claimed" page instead of 101 // the generic invalid-link error. 102 if (result.reason === "not_found" && context.session) { 103 const parentMatch = await parentMatchPromise 104 const userRegistrations = parentMatch.loaderData?.userRegistrations 105 const competition = parentMatch.loaderData?.competition 106 const firstRegistration = userRegistrations?.[0] 107 if (competition && firstRegistration) { 108 return { 109 kind: "already_claimed", 110 championshipName: competition.name, 111 registrationId: firstRegistration.id, 112 } 113 } 114 } 115 116 return { 117 kind: "invalid", 118 reason: result.reason, 119 championshipName: 120 "championshipName" in result ? result.championshipName : undefined, 121 } 122 } 123 124 const session = context.session ?? null 125 const match = identityMatch( 126 session?.user ? { email: session.user.email } : null, 127 result.invite, 128 { accountExistsForInviteEmail: result.accountExistsForInviteEmail }, 129 ) 130 131 if (match.ok) { 132 return { 133 kind: "claimable", 134 divisionId: result.invite.championshipDivisionId, 135 divisionLabel: result.divisionLabel, 136 championshipName: result.championshipName, 137 } 138 } 139 140 if (match.reason === "wrong_account") { 141 return { 142 kind: "wrong_account", 143 championshipName: result.championshipName, 144 inviteEmail: result.invite.email, 145 accountExistsForInviteEmail: result.accountExistsForInviteEmail, 146 } 147 } 148 149 // Signed out — redirect into sign-in / sign-up with email pre-filled and 150 // the invite token so the post-auth flow re-runs the claim. 151 const authPath = match.reason === "needs_sign_in" ? "/sign-in" : "/sign-up" 152 throw redirect({ 153 to: authPath, 154 search: { 155 redirect: `/compete/${params.slug}/claim/${params.token}`, 156 email: result.invite.email, 157 invite: params.token, 158 }, 159 }) 160 }, 161 }) 162 163 function ClaimPage() { 164 const { slug, token } = Route.useParams() 165 const data = Route.useLoaderData() 166 167 if (data.kind === "invalid") { 168 return ( 169 173 ) 174 } 175 176 if (data.kind === "wrong_account") { 177 return ( 178 185 ) 186 } 187 188 if (data.kind === "already_claimed") { 189 return ( 190 195 ) 196 } 197 198 if (data.kind === "over_allocated") { 199 return 200 } 201 202 return ( 203 210 ) 211 } 212 213 // ============================================================================ 214 // Branch components 215 // ============================================================================ 216 217 function ClaimablePage(props: { 218 slug: string 219 token: string 220 divisionId: string 221 divisionLabel: string 222 championshipName: string 223 }) { 224 return ( 225

712	            },
713	          })
714	          return {
715	            kind: "not_claimable" as const,
716	            reason: "not_found" as InviteClaimableError,
717	          }
718	        }

1	/**
2	 * Per-(source, championship-division) allocation resolution.
3	 *
4	 * ADR-0012 Phase 1: pure functional helper plus a DB read used by the
5	 * organizer-invites loader. The resolver applies the
6	 * default-plus-override pattern (source default → per-division override),
7	 * preserving the existing series math when no override is set.
8	 *
9	 * The resolver is intentionally pure — the caller passes in the source row,
10	 * the championship's divisions, and the allocation rows already loaded
11	 * from the DB. This keeps it easy to unit-test against ADR-0011's
12	 * roster fixtures without standing up a database.
13	 */
14	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Sources schema]]
15	
16	import "server-only"
17	
18	import { eq, inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
19	import { getDb } from "@/db"
20	import {
21	  COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND,
22	  type CompetitionInviteSource,
23	  type CompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocation,
24	  competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable,
25	  competitionInviteSourcesTable,
26	} from "@/db/schemas/competition-invites"
27	
28	// ============================================================================
29	// Types
30	// ============================================================================
31	
32	export interface ChampionshipDivision {
33	  id: string
34	  label: string
35	}
36	
37	export interface ResolvedSourceAllocations {
38	  /** Total contributed by this source across the championship's divisions. */
39	  total: number
40	  /** Per-championship-division breakdown — keyed by championshipDivisionId. */
41	  byDivision: Record<string, number>
42	}
43	
44	// ============================================================================
45	// Resolution
46	// ============================================================================
47	
48	/**
49	 * Default allocation for a championship division when no override row
50	 * exists for `(source.id, division.id)`.
51	 *
52	 * - `kind = "competition"`: the source's `globalSpots` (Top N qualifies),
53	 *   applied per division. Null collapses to 0.
54	 * - `kind = "series"`: `directSpotsPerComp * seriesCompCount + globalSpots`,
55	 *   applied per division. This preserves ADR-0011's series math while
56	 *   distributing it equally per division by default — when the organizer
57	 *   wants different per-division counts they create override rows.
58	 */
59	function sourceDefaultPerDivision(
60	  source: CompetitionInviteSource,
61	  seriesCompCount: number | undefined,
62	): number {
63	  const globalSpots = source.globalSpots ?? 0
64	  if (source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) {
65	    const direct = source.directSpotsPerComp ?? 0
66	    const compCount = seriesCompCount ?? 0
67	    return direct * compCount + globalSpots
68	  }
69	  return globalSpots
70	}
71	
72	/**
73	 * Resolve per-championship-division allocations for one source, applying
74	 * the default-plus-override pattern. Returns the per-division map plus the
75	 * total contributed by this source across the championship.
76	 *
77	 * `allocations` should already be filtered to rows for `source.id` (the
78	 * helper does not re-filter — passing rows for other sources is a caller
79	 * bug, not a resolver bug). Empty `championshipDivisions` returns
80	 * `{ total: 0, byDivision: {} }`.
81	 */
82	export function resolveSourceAllocations(args: {
83	  source: CompetitionInviteSource
84	  championshipDivisions: ReadonlyArray<ChampionshipDivision>
85	  allocations: ReadonlyArray<CompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocation>
86	  /** For series sources: how many comps in the series (so directSpotsPerComp
87	   *  scales). Ignored for single-comp sources. */
88	  seriesCompCount?: number
89	}): ResolvedSourceAllocations {
90	  const overrideByDivisionId = new Map<string, number>()
91	  for (const allocation of args.allocations) {
92	    if (allocation.sourceId !== args.source.id) continue
93	    overrideByDivisionId.set(allocation.championshipDivisionId, allocation.spots)
94	  }
95	
96	  const defaultSpots = sourceDefaultPerDivision(
97	    args.source,
98	    args.seriesCompCount,
99	  )
100	
101	  const byDivision: Record<string, number> = {}
102	  let total = 0
103	  for (const division of args.championshipDivisions) {
104	    const override = overrideByDivisionId.get(division.id)
105	    const spots = override !== undefined ? override : defaultSpots
106	    byDivision[division.id] = spots
107	    total += spots
108	  }
109	
110	  return { total, byDivision }
111	}
112	
113	// ============================================================================
114	// DB reads
115	// ============================================================================
116	
117	/**
118	 * Load all per-(source, championship-division) allocation rows for a
119	 * championship's sources. Returns one flat list — callers typically index
120	 * by `sourceId` themselves.
121	 *
122	 * Implemented as a join from `competition_invite_sources` rather than a
123	 * fan-out: one query, one result, the `championshipDivisionId` index on
124	 * the allocation table makes the per-division grouping cheap downstream.
125	 */
126	export async function listAllocationsForChampionship(args: {
127	  championshipCompetitionId: string
128	}): Promise<CompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocation[]> {
129	  const db = getDb()
130	
131	  const sourceIdRows = await db
132	    .select({ id: competitionInviteSourcesTable.id })
133	    .from(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
134	    .where(
135	      eq(
136	        competitionInviteSourcesTable.championshipCompetitionId,
137	        args.championshipCompetitionId,
138	      ),
139	    )
140	
141	  if (sourceIdRows.length === 0) return []
142	
143	  const sourceIds = sourceIdRows.map((r) => r.id)
144	
145	  return db
146	    .select()
147	    .from(competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable)
148	    .where(
149	      inArray(
150	        competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable.sourceId,
151	        sourceIds,
152	      ),
153	    )
154	}
155

1	import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest"
2	
3	// Two-row select-result queue. The bucket count helper fires two reads
4	// in parallel (accepted-paid invites + pending invite-driven purchases);
5	// the queue maps 1:1 to those reads in dispatch order.
6	const selectQueue: unknown[][] = []
7	
8	function makeChain(): unknown {
9		const chain: Record<string, unknown> = {}
10		const noop = () => chain
11		for (const m of ["from", "where", "innerJoin"]) {
12			chain[m] = vi.fn(noop)
13		}
14		chain.then = (resolve: (value: unknown) => void) => {
15			const next = selectQueue.shift() ?? []
16			resolve(next)
17			return Promise.resolve(next)
18		}
19		return chain
20	}
21	
22	const fakeDb = {
23		select: vi.fn(() => makeChain()),
24	}
25	
26	vi.mock("@/db", () => ({
27		getDb: vi.fn(() => fakeDb),
28	}))
29	
30	vi.mock("cloudflare:workers", () => ({
31		env: { APP_URL: "https://test.wodsmith.com" },
32	}))
33	
34	import { getOccupiedCountForBucket } from "@/server/competition-invites/claim"
35	
36	beforeEach(() => {
37		selectQueue.length = 0
38		fakeDb.select.mockClear()
39	})
40	
41	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Claim resolution]]
42	describe("getOccupiedCountForBucket", () => {
43		// Mirrors the regular division-capacity pattern: a slot in the
44		// (sourceId, championshipDivisionId) bucket is occupied when an
45		// invite is accepted_paid OR an athlete is mid-Stripe-checkout for
46		// an invite-driven purchase. Two athletes claiming the last spot
47		// must both see the in-flight hold so the second one bounces at
48		// claim load instead of paying-then-refunding.
49		it("sums accepted_paid invites and in-flight pending purchases in the bucket", async () => {
50			// Read 1: accepted_paid invites count.
51			selectQueue.push([{ count: 3 }])
52			// Read 2: pending invite-driven purchases count (held spots).
53			selectQueue.push([{ count: 2 }])
54	
55			const total = await getOccupiedCountForBucket({
56				sourceId: "cisrc_1",
57				championshipCompetitionId: "comp_champ",
58				championshipDivisionId: "div_rxm",
59			})
60	
61			expect(total).toBe(5)
62			expect(fakeDb.select).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)
63		})
64	
65		it("coerces string COUNT(*) values from the PlanetScale driver to numbers", async () => {
66			// PlanetScale returns COUNT(*) as a string in some shapes. The
67			// previous helper already used Number() — pin that the new helper
68			// keeps doing so for both component reads.
69			selectQueue.push([{ count: "4" }])
70			selectQueue.push([{ count: "1" }])
71	
72			const total = await getOccupiedCountForBucket({
73				sourceId: "cisrc_1",
74				championshipCompetitionId: "comp_champ",
75				championshipDivisionId: "div_rxm",
76			})
77	
78			expect(total).toBe(5)
79		})
80	
81		it("returns 0 when neither accepted nor pending rows exist for the bucket", async () => {
82			selectQueue.push([{ count: 0 }])
83			selectQueue.push([{ count: 0 }])
84	
85			const total = await getOccupiedCountForBucket({
86				sourceId: "cisrc_empty",
87				championshipCompetitionId: "comp_champ",
88				championshipDivisionId: "div_rxm",
89			})
90	
91			expect(total).toBe(0)
92		})
93	
94		// The exclude trick is what makes the webhook re-check work: the
95		// current purchase is still PENDING when the workflow probes the
96		// bucket, so the count must subtract it. Same `id != $purchaseId`
97		// pattern the regular division-capacity webhook uses.
98		it("accepts excludePurchaseId so the webhook can omit its own purchase row", async () => {
99			selectQueue.push([{ count: 1 }])
100			selectQueue.push([{ count: 0 }])
101	
102			const total = await getOccupiedCountForBucket({
103				sourceId: "cisrc_1",
104				championshipCompetitionId: "comp_champ",
105				championshipDivisionId: "div_rxm",
106				excludePurchaseId: "cmp_self",
107			})
108	
109			expect(total).toBe(1)
110		})
111	})
112

/**
 * Registration Server Functions for TanStack Start
 *
 * This file uses top-level imports for server-only modules.
 * Port of commerce.action.ts registration functions
 *
 * OBSERVABILITY:
 * - All registration operations are logged with request context
 * - Registration IDs, competition IDs, and payment info are tracked
 * - Payment flow states (pending, completed, cancelled) are logged
 */

import { createId } from "@paralleldrive/cuid2"
import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
import { and, eq, inArray, isNull, ne, or, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
import type Stripe from "stripe"
import { z } from "zod"
import { CLAIM_TOKEN_EXPIRATION_SECONDS } from "@/constants"
import { getDb } from "@/db"
import type { ProductCoupon } from "@/db/schema"
import {
  affiliatesTable,
  COMMERCE_PAYMENT_STATUS,
  COMMERCE_PRODUCT_TYPE,
  COMMERCE_PURCHASE_STATUS,
  commerceProductTable,
  commercePurchaseTable,
  competitionEventsTable,
  competitionHeatAssignmentsTable,
  competitionRegistrationAnswersTable,
  competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable,
  competitionRegistrationsTable,
  competitionsTable,
  createCommerceProductId,
  createCommercePurchaseId,
  createTeamId,
  createTeamMembershipId,
  createUserId,
  REGISTRATION_STATUS,
  ROLES_ENUM,
  scalingGroupsTable,
  scalingLevelsTable,
  scoresTable,
  TEAM_PERMISSIONS,
  teamInvitationTable,
  teamMembershipTable,
  teamTable,
  userTable,
} from "@/db/schema"
import {
  buildFeeConfig,
  calculateCompetitionFees,
  type FeeBreakdown,
  getRegistrationFee,
  type TeamFeeOverrides,
} from "@/lib/commerce-stubs"
import { getAppUrl } from "@/lib/env"
import { getEvlog } from "@/lib/evlog"
import {
  addRequestContextAttribute,
  logEntityCreated,
  logInfo,
  logWarning,
  updateRequestContext,
} from "@/lib/logging"
import {
  notifyRegistrationConfirmed,
  registerForCompetition,
} from "@/lib/registration-stubs"
import { getStripe } from "@/lib/stripe"
import {
  COMPETITION_INVITE_STATUS,
  competitionInvitesTable,
} from "@/db/schemas/competition-invites"
import {
  assertInviteClaimable,
  findActiveInviteForEmail,
  resolveInviteForClaim,
} from "@/server/competition-invites/claim"
import { normalizeInviteEmail } from "@/server/competition-invites/issue"
import { recordRedemption, validateCoupon } from "@/server/coupons"
import { requireVerifiedEmail } from "@/utils/auth"
import { createToken, getClaimTokenKey } from "@/utils/auth-utils"
import {
  DEFAULT_TIMEZONE,
  hasDateStartedInTimezone,
  isDeadlinePassedInTimezone,
} from "@/utils/timezone-utils"
import {
  getCompetitionSpotsAvailableFn,
  getDivisionSpotsAvailableFn,
} from "./competition-divisions-fns"

// ============================================================================
// Input Schemas
// ============================================================================

const registrationItemSchema = z.object({
  divisionId: z.string().min(1, "Division ID is required"),
  teamName: z.string().optional(),
  teammates: z
    .array(
      z.object({
        email: z.string().email(),
        firstName: z.string().optional(),
        lastName: z.string().optional(),
        affiliateName: z.string().max(255).optional(),
      }),
    )
    .optional(),
})

const initiateRegistrationPaymentInputSchema = z.object({
  competitionId: z.string().min(1, "Competition ID is required"),
  // Multi-division support: array of division entries
  items: z
    .array(registrationItemSchema)
    .min(1, "At least one division is required")
    .refine(
      (items) => new Set(items.map((i) => i.divisionId)).size === items.length,
      { message: "Duplicate division selections are not allowed" },
    ),
  // Shared fields
  affiliateName: z.string().max(255).optional(),
  couponCode: z.string().max(100).optional(),
  answers: z
    .array(
      z.object({
        questionId: z.string().min(1),
        answer: z.string().max(5000),
      }),
    )
    .optional(),
  /**
   * Optional competition-invite claim token. When present:
   *  - re-validates the invite (email-locked to the session),
   *  - skips the registration-window check (invite-holders can register
   *    before public open / after close),
   *  - tags the Stripe purchase metadata with `inviteId` so the webhook
   *    workflow can flip the invite to `accepted_paid`.
   */
  inviteToken: z.string().min(1).optional(),
})

const getRegistrationFeeBreakdownInputSchema = z.object({
  competitionId: z.string().min(1, "Competition ID is required"),
  divisionId: z.string().min(1, "Division ID is required"),
})

const getUserCompetitionRegistrationInputSchema = z.object({
  competitionId: z.string().min(1, "Competition ID is required"),
  userId: z.string().min(1, "User ID is required"),
})

// ============================================================================
// Helper Functions
// ============================================================================

/**
 * Validate that all required questions have answers
 * Throws error if required questions are missing
 */
async function validateRequiredQuestions(
  competitionId: string,
  answers: Array<{ questionId: string; answer: string }> | undefined,
): Promise<void> {
  const db = getDb()

  // Look up the competition's groupId
  const [competition] = await db

content.content:
100	  id: string
101	  label: string
102	}
103	
104	/**
105	 * Parse a source's `divisionMappings` JSON column into a typed array.
106	 *
107	 * The column is `text` storing
108	 * `[{ sourceDivisionId, championshipDivisionId, spots? }]`. Returns an
109	 * empty array on null / malformed JSON — the caller falls back to label
110	 * matching, which is the common case for organizers who haven't filled
111	 * in mappings yet.
112	 */
113	function parseDivisionMappings(
114	  raw: string | null | undefined,
115	): DivisionMapping[] {
116	  if (!raw) return []
117	  try {
118	    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw)
119	    if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return []
120	    return parsed.filter(
121	      (m): m is DivisionMapping =>
122	        typeof m === "object" &&
123	        m !== null &&
124	        typeof (m as { sourceDivisionId?: unknown }).sourceDivisionId ===
125	          "string" &&
126	        typeof (m as { championshipDivisionId?: unknown })
127	          .championshipDivisionId === "string",
128	    )
129	  } catch {
130	    return []
131	  }
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Map a source-side division id to its championship-division counterpart.
136	 *
137	 * Resolution order:
138	 *   1. Explicit row in the source's `divisionMappings` JSON.
139	 *   2. Label match against the championship's divisions
140	 *      (case-insensitive, trimmed) — covers the common case where the
141	 *      source comp uses the same division labels as the championship.
142	 *
143	 * Returns `null` when no mapping can be inferred. Callers use that as
144	 * the signal to skip the cutoff (i.e. include all leaderboard rows) so
145	 * mis-mapped divisions don't silently drop qualifying athletes.
146	 */
147	function mapSourceDivisionToChampionship(args: {
148	  sourceDivisionId: string
149	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
150	  divisionMappings: DivisionMapping[]
151	  championshipDivisions: ReadonlyArray<ChampionshipDivisionInfo>
152	}): string | null {
153	  const explicit = args.divisionMappings.find(
154	    (m) => m.sourceDivisionId === args.sourceDivisionId,
155	  )
156	  if (explicit) return explicit.championshipDivisionId
157	
158	  const normalized = args.sourceDivisionLabel.trim().toLowerCase()
159	  const matched = args.championshipDivisions.find(
160	    (d) => d.label.trim().toLowerCase() === normalized,
161	  )
162	  return matched ? matched.id : null
163	}
164	
165	/**
166	 * Resolve the (competitionId, name) tuples each source contributes:
167	 * single-comp source → 1 row; series source → every comp in the group.
168	 *
169	 * Returns a deduped list keyed by `competitionId` so a comp referenced
170	 * by both a direct source AND a series source only fetches once. The
171	 * first-encountered source wins for tagging.
172	 */
173	async function resolveSourceCompetitions(
174	  championshipId: string,
175	): Promise<{
176	  sources: CompetitionInviteSource[]
177	  refs: SourceCompetitionRef[]
178	  seriesCompCountBySourceId: Map<string, number>
179	}> {
180	  const sources = await listSourcesForChampionship(championshipId)
181	  if (sources.length === 0) {
182	    return { sources: [], refs: [], seriesCompCountBySourceId: new Map() }
183	  }
184	
185	  const db = getDb()
186	
187	  // Collect all comp ids we need to look up.
188	  const directIds = sources
189	    .filter(
190	      (s): s is typeof s & { sourceCompetitionId: string } =>
191	        s.kind === "competition" && !!s.sourceCompetitionId,
192	    )
193	    .map((s) => s.sourceCompetitionId)
194	  const groupIds = sources
195	    .filter(
196	      (s): s is typeof s & { sourceGroupId: string } =>
197	        s.kind === "series" && !!s.sourceGroupId,
198	    )
199	    .map((s) => s.sourceGroupId)
200	
201	  // One query per category; each can be empty.
202	  const [directComps, seriesComps] = await Promise.all([
203	    directIds.length > 0
204	      ? db
205	          .select({
206	            id: competitionsTable.id,
207	            name: competitionsTable.name,
208	            groupId: competitionsTable.groupId,
209	          })
210	          .from(competitionsTable)
211	          .where(inArray(competitionsTable.id, directIds))
212	      : Promise.resolve(
213	          [] as Array<{ id: string; name: string; groupId: string | null }>,
214	        ),
215	    groupIds.length > 0
216	      ? db
217	          .select({
218	            id: competitionsTable.id,
219	            name: competitionsTable.name,
220	            groupId: competitionsTable.groupId,
221	          })
222	          .from(competitionsTable)
223	          .where(inArray(competitionsTable.groupId, groupIds))
224	      : Promise.resolve(
225	          [] as Array<{ id: string; name: string; groupId: string | null }>,
226	        ),
227	  ])
228	
229	  const refs: SourceCompetitionRef[] = []
230	  const seen = new Set<string>()
231	  const seriesCompCountBySourceId = new Map<string, number>()
232	
233	  for (const source of sources) {
234	    if (source.kind === "competition" && source.sourceCompetitionId) {
235	      if (seen.has(source.sourceCompetitionId)) continue
236	      const row = directComps.find((c) => c.id === source.sourceCompetitionId)
237	      if (!row) continue
238	      seen.add(source.sourceCompetitionId)
239	      refs.push({
240	        sourceId: source.id,
241	        sourceKind: source.kind,
242	        competitionId: row.id,
243	        competitionName: row.name,
244	      })
245	    } else if (source.kind === "series" && source.sourceGroupId) {
246	      const matchingComps = seriesComps.filter(
247	        (c) => c.groupId === source.sourceGroupId,
248	      )
249	      seriesCompCountBySourceId.set(source.id, matchingComps.length)
250	      for (const c of matchingComps) {
251	        if (seen.has(c.id)) continue
252	        seen.add(c.id)
253	        refs.push({
254	          sourceId: source.id,
255	          sourceKind: source.kind,
256	          competitionId: c.id,
257	          competitionName: c.name,
258	        })
259	      }
260	    }
261	  }
262	  return { sources, refs, seriesCompCountBySourceId }
263	}
264	
265	/**
266	 * Resolve every (competition, division) pair to fetch a leaderboard
267	 * for. Reads `competitionsTable.settings` to find each comp's scaling
268	 * group, then resolves levels in one batched query.
269	 */

commit f9a67c15efb632b0fc8e6ef678037472a004f466
Author: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Date:   Wed Apr 29 04:49:04 2026 +0000

    feat(invites): per-division global spots for series qualification sources
    
    Series sources now support per-division overrides on the global-leaderboard
    portion (e.g. 3 RX globals, 2 Scaled globals) in addition to the existing
    absolute-total override. Both axes live on the same allocation row keyed by
    (sourceId, championshipDivisionId); the source details page stacks a new
    "Per-division global spots" card above the existing per-division allocation
    table for series sources, mirroring the same Use-default + numeric-input UX.
    
    The resolver applies precedence per row: spots non-null → absolute total
    override; else globalSpots non-null → directSpotsPerComp*compCount + globalSpots;
    else → source default. Single-comp sources are unchanged in practice (UI
    only exposes the new card for series).

content.content:
100	export type CompetitionInviteSource = InferSelectModel<
101	  typeof competitionInviteSourcesTable
102	>
103	
104	// ============================================================================
105	// Source × Division Allocations
106	// ============================================================================
107	
108	/**
109	 * Per-(source, championship-division) override of the invite-spot allocation.
110	 *
111	 * Per ADR-0012: the source row's `globalSpots` / `directSpotsPerComp` are the
112	 * defaults; a row in this table means "this championship division differs
113	 * from the default for this source." A `spots` of `0` is meaningful — it
114	 * pins the division to zero from this source. Absence of the row means
115	 * "use the source default."
116	 *
117	 * Cascading delete on the source row is handled in
118	 * `apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/sources.ts`
119	 * (`deleteSource`) — no FKs per PlanetScale convention.
120	 */
121	export const competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable = mysqlTable(
122	  "competition_invite_source_division_allocations",
123	  {
124	    ...commonColumns,
125	    id: varchar({ length: 255 })
126	      .primaryKey()
127	      .$defaultFn(() => createCompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationId())
128	      .notNull(),
129	    // Refs `competition_invite_sources.id` (no FK; cascade in helper).
130	    sourceId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
131	    // Refs `competition_divisions.id` (scaling level id) of the championship.
132	    championshipDivisionId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
133	    // Override allocation count. 0 means "this division gets none from this
134	    // source"; absence of the row means "use the source default."
135	    spots: int().notNull(),
136	  },
137	  (table) => [
138	    uniqueIndex(
139	      "competition_invite_source_division_allocations_source_div_unique",
140	    ).on(table.sourceId, table.championshipDivisionId),
141	    index("competition_invite_source_division_allocations_div_idx").on(
142	      table.championshipDivisionId,
143	    ),
144	  ],
145	)
146	
147	export type CompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocation = InferSelectModel<
148	  typeof competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable
149	>
150	
151	// ============================================================================
152	// Invite Origin
153	// ============================================================================
154	
155	export const COMPETITION_INVITE_ORIGIN = {
156	  SOURCE: "source",
157	  BESPOKE: "bespoke",
158	} as const
159	
160	export type CompetitionInviteOrigin =
161	  (typeof COMPETITION_INVITE_ORIGIN)[keyof typeof COMPETITION_INVITE_ORIGIN]
162	
163	// ============================================================================
164	// Invite Status
165	// ============================================================================
166	
167	/**
168	 * `pending` — invite issued, token live, waiting for the athlete to claim.
169	 * `accepted_paid` — the athlete finished Stripe Checkout and their
170	 *   `competition_registrations` row exists; set by the Stripe workflow.
171	 * `declined` — athlete explicitly declined via the decline route.
172	 * `expired` — cron-sweep transitioned a past-deadline pending invite.
173	 * `revoked` — organizer or an R2 send revoked the R1 invite.
174	 *
175	 * No intermediate `accepted` state — the Stripe-in-flight window is still
176	 * `pending` until the webhook confirms payment. See ADR-0011 "Capacity Math".
177	 */
178	export const COMPETITION_INVITE_STATUS = {
179	  PENDING: "pending",
180	  ACCEPTED_PAID: "accepted_paid",
181	  DECLINED: "declined",
182	  EXPIRED: "expired",
183	  REVOKED: "revoked",
184	} as const
185	
186	export type CompetitionInviteStatus =
187	  (typeof COMPETITION_INVITE_STATUS)[keyof typeof COMPETITION_INVITE_STATUS]
188	
189	// ============================================================================
190	// Invite Email Delivery Status
191	// ============================================================================
192	
193	export const COMPETITION_INVITE_EMAIL_DELIVERY_STATUS = {
194	  QUEUED: "queued",
195	  SENT: "sent",
196	  FAILED: "failed",
197	  SKIPPED: "skipped",
198	} as const
199

aiTitle: Debug invite allocation override enforcement bug

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Invite Source details page — ADR-0012 Phase 3.
3	 *
4	 * Hosts source meta editing (kind, source comp/series, default spots,
5	 * notes) plus the per-(championship division) allocation table where the
6	 * organizer overrides the source's default for specific divisions.
7	 *
8	 * The Sources tab's "Edit" button navigates here; the Add flow keeps the
9	 * existing dialog because there are no overrides to edit at create time.
10	 */
11	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Source details page]]
12	
13	import {
14	  createFileRoute,
15	  Link,
16	  redirect,
17	  useNavigate,
18	  useRouter,
19	} from "@tanstack/react-router"
20	import { useServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
21	import { ArrowLeft } from "lucide-react"
22	import { useMemo, useState } from "react"
23	import { toast } from "sonner"
24	import {
25	  InviteSourceForm,
26	  type InviteSourceFormValues,
27	} from "@/components/organizer/invites/invite-source-form"
28	import { Alert, AlertDescription } from "@/components/ui/alert"
29	import {
30	  Breadcrumb,
31	  BreadcrumbItem,
32	  BreadcrumbLink,
33	  BreadcrumbList,
34	  BreadcrumbPage,
35	  BreadcrumbSeparator,
36	} from "@/components/ui/breadcrumb"
37	import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"
38	import {
39	  Card,
40	  CardContent,
41	  CardDescription,
42	  CardHeader,
43	  CardTitle,
44	} from "@/components/ui/card"
45	import { Checkbox } from "@/components/ui/checkbox"
46	import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input"
47	import {
48	  Table,
49	  TableBody,
50	  TableCell,
51	  TableHead,
52	  TableHeader,
53	  TableRow,
54	} from "@/components/ui/table"
55	import { getCompetitionDivisionsWithCountsFn } from "@/server-fns/competition-divisions-fns"
56	import {
57	  getCompetitionGroupsFn,
58	  getOrganizerCompetitionsFn,
59	} from "@/server-fns/competition-fns"
60	import {
61	  getInviteSourceByIdFn,
62	  listInviteSourceAllocationsFn,
63	  saveInviteSourceAllocationsFn,
64	  updateInviteSourceFn,
65	} from "@/server-fns/competition-invite-fns"
66	
67	export const Route = createFileRoute(
68	  "/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/sources/$sourceId",
69	)({
70	  staleTime: 10_000,
71	  component: InviteSourceDetailsPage,
72	  loader: async ({ params, context, parentMatchPromise }) => {
73	    const session = context.session
74	    if (!session?.user?.id) {
75	      throw redirect({
76	        to: "/sign-in",
77	        search: {
78	          redirect: `/compete/organizer/${params.competitionId}/invites/sources/${params.sourceId}`,
79	        },
80	      })
81	    }
82	
83	    const parentMatch = await parentMatchPromise
84	    const { competition } = parentMatch.loaderData!
85	
86	    // `getInviteSourceByIdFn` enforces MANAGE_COMPETITIONS on the source's
87	    // championship organizing team and throws when the source does not
88	    // exist — the parent error boundary handles both consistently.
89	    const [
90	      sourceResult,
91	      divisionsResult,
92	      allocationsResult,
93	      organizerCompsResult,
94	      organizerGroupsResult,
95	    ] = await Promise.all([
96	      getInviteSourceByIdFn({ data: { sourceId: params.sourceId } }),
97	      getCompetitionDivisionsWithCountsFn({
98	        data: {
99	          competitionId: params.competitionId,
100	          teamId: competition.organizingTeamId,
101	        },
102	      }),
103	      // The route-wide allocation map; we slice out this source's row
104	      // for the override table seed values.
105	      listInviteSourceAllocationsFn({
106	        data: { championshipCompetitionId: params.competitionId },
107	      }),
108	      getOrganizerCompetitionsFn({
109	        data: { teamId: competition.organizingTeamId },
110	      }),
111	      getCompetitionGroupsFn({
112	        data: { teamId: competition.organizingTeamId },
113	      }),
114	    ])
115	
116	    // Cross-check: a source whose championship doesn't match this URL
117	    // should not render. The server fn already gates by championship
118	    // permission via `getCompetitionOrganizingTeamId`, but a URL like
119	    // `/compete/organizer/A/invites/sources/<sourceForB>` is a typo we
120	    // can stop early at the loader.

170 const saveAllocations = useServerFn(saveInviteSourceAllocationsFn) 171 172 const [metaError, setMetaError] = useState(null) 173 const [allocationError, setAllocationError] = useState(null) 174 const [savingAllocations, setSavingAllocations] = useState(false) 175 176 // Source default applied per-division when no override row exists. 177 // Mirrors `sourceDefaultPerDivision` in the server-side allocations 178 // helper — kept simple here because the details page only needs the 179 // displayable number, not the full resolution algorithm. 180 const sourceDefaultPerDivision = useMemo(() => { 181 if (source.kind === "series") { 182 // Series default is `directSpotsPerComp * compCount + globalSpots` 183 // applied per-division. We only have the resolved per-division 184 // total in `allocationsBySourceByDivision[source.id]` — pick any 185 // entry where there's no override to derive the default. If we 186 // can't (every entry has an override), fall back to the raw 187 // globalSpots so the toggle copy still has a number to show. 188 const map = allocationsBySourceByDivision[source.id] ?? {} 189 const firstDefault = Object.values(map)[0] 190 if (typeof firstDefault === "number") return firstDefault 191 return source.globalSpots ?? 0 192 } 193 return source.globalSpots ?? 0 194 }, [source, allocationsBySourceByDivision]) 195 196 // Seed the per-division override map from the raw allocation rows. 197 // Presence of a row in `rawAllocationsForSource` means "override is 198 // active for this division" (toggle off, spots = row.spots). Absence 199 // means "uses source default" (toggle on, spots = source default for 200 // display only). 201 const overridesByDivisionId = useMemo(() => { 202 const map = new Map() 203 for (const row of rawAllocationsForSource) { 204 map.set(row.championshipDivisionId, row.spots) 205 } 206 return map 207 }, [rawAllocationsForSource]) 208 209 const initialOverrides = useMemo>(() => { 210 const out: Record = {} 211 for (const division of championshipDivisions) { 212 const overrideSpots = overridesByDivisionId.get(division.id) 213 if (overrideSpots !== undefined) { 214 out[division.id] = { 215 useDefault: false, 216 spots: String(overrideSpots), 217 } 218 } else { 219 out[division.id] = { 220 useDefault: true, 221 spots: String(sourceDefaultPerDivision), 222 } 223 } 224 } 225 return out 226 }, [championshipDivisions, overridesByDivisionId, sourceDefaultPerDivision]) 227 228 const [overrides, setOverrides] = 229 useState>(initialOverrides) 230 231 const setRow = (divisionId: string, patch: Partial) => { 232 setOverrides((prev) => ({ 233 ...prev, 234 [divisionId]: { ...prev[divisionId], ...patch }, 235 })) 236 } 237 238 const onMetaSubmit = async (values: InviteSourceFormValues) => { 239 setMetaError(null) 240 try { 241 await updateSource({ 242 data: { 243 id: source.id, 244 championshipCompetitionId: competitionId, 245 kind: values.kind, 246 sourceCompetitionId: 247 values.kind === "competition" ? values.sourceCompetitionId : null, 248 sourceGroupId: 249 values.kind === "series" ? values.sourceGroupId : null, 250 directSpotsPerComp: 251 values.kind === "series" 252 ? aiTitle: Debug invite allocation override enforcement bug

content.text:
I have enough to explain what's happening. Let me summarize the findings.

## Investigation summary

**The token in your URL resolves to a real invite, but the allocation gate is correctly letting it through given the data on `demo`.** The bug you describe (extras claiming spots past the override) doesn't match what's stored — **there is no override row for the source × division you're testing.** There is also a real, separate gap on the `register` server fn, but it isn't what's biting you here.

### What the demo data actually says

Token `18-gP4SQk…` → `cinv_01KQH26JP7RH0FBSEXRYE2YMRW` (zac+invite2@…), bucket = `(cisrc_01KQBQS6MK84X523A95BBT2AS7, slvl_inv_champ_mrx)`.

Source row (`competition_invite_sources`): `kind=series`, `direct_spots_per_comp=2`, `global_spots=1`, `source_group_id=cgrp_box1_throwdowns_2025` (2 comps in the series).

Allocation rows (`competition_invite_source_division_allocations`) for this source: **none.** No override exists for any division.

So `resolveAllocationForInvite` falls through to the default at `apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts:59` → `direct * compCount + global = 2*2 + 1 = 5 per division`.

Bucket occupancy: 2 `accepted_paid` invites (zac+invite1, ian+invite1), 0 in-flight pending purchases → `occupiedCount = 2`. `assertInviteWithinAllocation` → `2 < 5` → **passes**, by design.

If you intended a cap of 2 (or 1) for `slvl_inv_champ_mrx` from this series source, it was never saved — toggle the row off in the source-detail "Per-division allocation" table at `apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/sources/$sourceId.tsx:394` and enter the override. (The other sources on this championship — `cisrc_01KQ944…` and `cisrc_01KQGWX…` — do have override rows; the series source is the only one missing them.)

### Likely UX confusion: "default" is per-division, not total

Source-detail UI literally says "Default is **5** per division" for this source (`apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/sources/$sourceId.tsx:399`). For a series with `direct=2/comp, 2 comps, global=1`, that's `5 spots in each` championship division (MRX, WRX, Team RX, Team SC = 20 total). If you read `global_spots=1` as "1 wildcard across the whole championship," the system instead replicates it into every division. That's the documented behavior (`allocations.ts:54-69`) but it's the easiest way to misread the cap.

### Real gap (separate from the override question)

`initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` does **not** run the allocation guardrail. The flow is:

| Surface | Allocation check? |
| --- | --- |
| `/claim/$token` loader → `getInviteByTokenFn` | yes (`competition-invite-fns.ts:799-830`) |
| `/register?invite=…` loader (`register.tsx:270-473`) | **no** |
| `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` submit (`registration-fns.ts:295-393`) | **no** — only existing-reg + division/competition capacity |
| Stripe webhook (`stripe-checkout-workflow.ts:484-588`) | yes (authoritative; refunds if over-allocated) |

So an athlete who lands on `/register?invite=…&divisionId=…` directly (bookmark, refresh, or after sign-in redirect) skips the claim soft gate entirely. If the bucket is actually full, they reach Stripe, pay, and only the webhook stops them — by marking the purchase failed and refunding (`stripe-checkout-workflow.ts:524-588`). No registration is created, but the athlete sees a charge-then-refund. That's a clear UX bug to mirror the claim gate inside `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` before creating the Stripe session.

Note that with allocation=5 / accepted=2 in your current data, even fixing this gap wouldn't block zac+invite2 — the cap genuinely isn't reached.

### What I'd verify next

1. Confirm what override (if any) you intended on source `cisrc_01KQBQS6MK84X523A95BBT2AS7` for `slvl_inv_champ_mrx`. If the form looked saved but the row is missing, that's a separate save-path bug to chase in `saveInviteSourceAllocationsFn`.
2. Decide whether to add the allocation guardrail inside `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` (mirror the `if (invite.sourceId)` block from `competition-invite-fns.ts:799-830`) so the register-direct path can't reach Stripe when the bucket is full.
3. If "default per division" is consistently confusing organizers, consider a clearer label on the source form (e.g. show resolved per-division and championship-wide totals together).

Want me to implement #2 — port the allocation guardrail into `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` so the `/register` direct path matches the claim landing?

There's no complicated logic here. If Online Qualifier 2026 has top one finisher qualifies as the default all divisions from that that competition only get one invite that can take a spot at the competition If there is a per division allocation, then only say that Men's Rx is set to 2. Only two spots from online qualifier 2026 can be taken at this competition. There's no fancy math. There's no multiplying it by different competitions and adding a global spot for online qualified 2026, it's either one, which is the default, or Men's RX, which has two.

content.text:
Got it — that's a much simpler model than what's coded. The implementation today disagrees with what you described, and that *is* the bug for your `cisrc_01KQBQS6MK84X523A95BBT2AS7` case.

## What the code does today vs what you described

`apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts:59-70`:

```ts
function sourceDefaultPerDivision(source, seriesCompCount) {
  const globalSpots = source.globalSpots ?? 0
  if (source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) {
    const direct = source.directSpotsPerComp ?? 0
    const compCount = seriesCompCount ?? 0
    return direct * compCount + globalSpots   // ← the "fancy math"
  }
  return globalSpots
}
```

For your series source (`direct=2, global=1, 2 comps`) that returns `2*2 + 1 = 5` per division. With your `global_spots=1` ("top one finisher qualifies"), the math you described should return **1**. Override on Men's RX = 2 means 2. That's it.

## Surgical fix

Collapse `sourceDefaultPerDivision` to just `globalSpots ?? 0` for both kinds. The series branch and `directSpotsPerComp` go away. Concretely:

- `allocations.ts` `sourceDefaultPerDivision`: drop the series branch, return `globalSpots ?? 0`.
- Source form / source-detail page: remove the `directSpotsPerComp` input (and remove the field from `updateSourceFn` / `createSourceFn` schemas, or pin it to `null`).
- Schema column `direct_spots_per_comp`: keep nullable for now (drop in a follow-up migration) or null it out everywhere — your call.
- Tests: `allocations.test.ts`, `roster-allocations.test.ts` need their series-math expectations updated.
- Docs: `lat.md/competition-invites.md` "Allocations helper" needs to drop the series-math sentence.

Effect on your live data once deployed: `cisrc_01KQBQS6MK84X523A95BBT2AS7`'s default for `slvl_inv_champ_mrx` becomes **1**. Two are already `accepted_paid`, so zac+invite2's claim would correctly bounce with `over_allocated`.

## One question before I touch it

Two options for `direct_spots_per_comp`:

1. **Leave the column, ignore it.** Quickest. Existing rows untouched; resolver just reads `global_spots`. Risk: stale field in the schema confuses future readers.
2. **Remove it everywhere** — UI form, server-fn schemas, DB column (via `pnpm db:generate`). Cleaner; one extra migration step.

Which do you want? Once that's settled I'll also mirror the allocation guardrail into `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` so the `/register?invite=…` direct path can't bypass the gate.

[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Sources schema]] (lat.md/competition-invites.md:9-14)

> ## Sources schema
>
> A qualification source attached to a championship competition. Each row references either a single competition or a series (never both); allocation and division-mapping columns describe how many invitees the source contributes.
>
> Defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInviteSourcesTable]] with three indexes — `(championshipCompetitionId, sortOrder)` drives the Sources tab list, `(sourceCompetitionId)` lets us find every championship a given source competition feeds, and `(sourceGroupId)` does the same for series. ULID primary keys generated via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteSourceId]]. No FKs (PlanetScale convention).
>

1	/**
2	 * Competition Invite Sources — CRUD helpers
3	 *
4	 * Business-logic layer for `competition_invite_sources`. Each championship
5	 * competition has 0+ sources; each source is either a single-competition
6	 * reference or a series reference, never both. Division mappings and spot
7	 * allocations are stored per source.
8	 *
9	 * Write-time constraint: exactly one of `sourceCompetitionId` /
10	 * `sourceGroupId` is non-null per row. Enforced here, not at the DB
11	 * level, because PlanetScale does not support check constraints and we
12	 * want a consistent validation boundary that yields typed errors.
13	 */
14	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Sources helpers]]
15	
16	import { and, asc, eq } from "drizzle-orm"
17	import { getDb } from "@/db"
18	import {
19	  COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND,
20	  type CompetitionInviteSource,
21	  type CompetitionInviteSourceKind,
22	  competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable,
23	  competitionInviteSourcesTable,
24	} from "@/db/schemas/competition-invites"
25	
26	// ============================================================================
27	// Types
28	// ============================================================================
29	
30	export interface DivisionMapping {
31	  sourceDivisionId: string
32	  championshipDivisionId: string
33	  spots?: number | null
34	}
35	
36	export interface CreateSourceInput {
37	  championshipCompetitionId: string
38	  kind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
39	  sourceCompetitionId?: string | null
40	  sourceGroupId?: string | null
41	  directSpotsPerComp?: number | null
42	  globalSpots?: number | null
43	  divisionMappings?: DivisionMapping[] | null
44	  sortOrder?: number
45	  notes?: string | null
46	}
47	
48	export interface UpdateSourceInput {
49	  id: string
50	  kind?: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
51	  sourceCompetitionId?: string | null
52	  sourceGroupId?: string | null
53	  directSpotsPerComp?: number | null
54	  globalSpots?: number | null
55	  divisionMappings?: DivisionMapping[] | null
56	  sortOrder?: number
57	  notes?: string | null
58	}
59	
60	export class InviteSourceValidationError extends Error {
61	  constructor(message: string) {
62	    super(message)
63	    this.name = "InviteSourceValidationError"
64	  }
65	}
66	
67	// ============================================================================
68	// Validation
69	// ============================================================================
70	
71	/**
72	 * Enforce the "exactly one of sourceCompetitionId / sourceGroupId" constraint
73	 * and the `kind` alignment with which column is populated. Throws an
74	 * `InviteSourceValidationError` on violation.
75	 *
76	 * A `kind = "series"` source with no `globalSpots` / `directSpotsPerComp`
77	 * set is intentionally allowed — the organizer may want to set them later
78	 * before actually sourcing invitees.
79	 */
80	export function assertSourceReferenceValid(input: {
81	  kind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
82	  sourceCompetitionId?: string | null
83	  sourceGroupId?: string | null
84	}): void {
85	  const hasComp = !!input.sourceCompetitionId
86	  const hasGroup = !!input.sourceGroupId
87	
88	  if (hasComp && hasGroup) {
89	    throw new InviteSourceValidationError(
90	      "Source cannot reference both a competition and a series",
91	    )
92	  }
93	  if (!hasComp && !hasGroup) {
94	    throw new InviteSourceValidationError(
95	      "Source must reference exactly one of a competition or a series",
96	    )
97	  }
98	  if (input.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.COMPETITION && !hasComp) {
99	    throw new InviteSourceValidationError(
100	      'kind "competition" requires sourceCompetitionId',
101	    )
102	  }
103	  if (input.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES && !hasGroup) {
104	    throw new InviteSourceValidationError(
105	      'kind "series" requires sourceGroupId',
106	    )
107	  }
108	}
109	
110	// ============================================================================
111	// Queries
112	// ============================================================================
113	
114	export async function listSourcesForChampionship(
115	  championshipCompetitionId: string,
116	): Promise<CompetitionInviteSource[]> {
117	  const db = getDb()
118	  return db
119	    .select()
120	    .from(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
121	    .where(
122	      eq(
123	        competitionInviteSourcesTable.championshipCompetitionId,
124	        championshipCompetitionId,
125	      ),
126	    )
127	    .orderBy(
128	      asc(competitionInviteSourcesTable.sortOrder),
129	      asc(competitionInviteSourcesTable.createdAt),
130	    )
131	}
132	
133	export async function getSourceById(
134	  id: string,
135	): Promise<CompetitionInviteSource | null> {
136	  const db = getDb()
137	  const [row] = await db
138	    .select()
139	    .from(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
140	    .where(eq(competitionInviteSourcesTable.id, id))
141	    .limit(1)
142	  return row ?? null
143	}
144	
145	// ============================================================================
146	// Mutations
147	// ============================================================================
148	
149	function serializeDivisionMappings(
150	  mappings: DivisionMapping[] | null | undefined,
151	): string | null {
152	  if (!mappings || mappings.length === 0) return null
153	  return JSON.stringify(mappings)
154	}
155	
156	export async function createSource(
157	  input: CreateSourceInput,
158	): Promise<CompetitionInviteSource> {
159	  assertSourceReferenceValid({
160	    kind: input.kind,
161	    sourceCompetitionId: input.sourceCompetitionId,
162	    sourceGroupId: input.sourceGroupId,
163	  })
164	
165	  const db = getDb()
166	  const values = {
167	    championshipCompetitionId: input.championshipCompetitionId,
168	    kind: input.kind,
169	    sourceCompetitionId: input.sourceCompetitionId ?? null,
170	    sourceGroupId: input.sourceGroupId ?? null,
171	    directSpotsPerComp: input.directSpotsPerComp ?? null,
172	    globalSpots: input.globalSpots ?? null,
173	    divisionMappings: serializeDivisionMappings(input.divisionMappings),
174	    sortOrder: input.sortOrder ?? 0,
175	    notes: input.notes ?? null,
176	  }
177	
178	  const [inserted] = await db
179	    .insert(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
180	    .values(values)
181	    .$returningId()
182	
183	  // MySQL insert returns only the id; re-read for canonical shape.
184	  const row = await getSourceById(inserted.id)
185	  if (!row) {
186	    throw new Error("Source was inserted but could not be re-read")
187	  }
188	  return row
189	}
190	
191	export async function updateSource(
192	  input: UpdateSourceInput,
193	): Promise<CompetitionInviteSource> {
194	  const existing = await getSourceById(input.id)
195	  if (!existing) {
196	    throw new InviteSourceValidationError("Source not found")
197	  }
198	
199	  const nextKind = input.kind ?? existing.kind
200	  const nextComp =
201	    input.sourceCompetitionId !== undefined
202	      ? input.sourceCompetitionId
203	      : existing.sourceCompetitionId
204	  const nextGroup =
205	    input.sourceGroupId !== undefined
206	      ? input.sourceGroupId
207	      : existing.sourceGroupId
208	
209	  assertSourceReferenceValid({
210	    kind: nextKind,
211	    sourceCompetitionId: nextComp,
212	    sourceGroupId: nextGroup,
213	  })
214	
215	  const db = getDb()
216	  const patch: Record<string, unknown> = {}
217	  if (input.kind !== undefined) patch.kind = input.kind
218	  if (input.sourceCompetitionId !== undefined)
219	    patch.sourceCompetitionId = input.sourceCompetitionId
220	  if (input.sourceGroupId !== undefined)
221	    patch.sourceGroupId = input.sourceGroupId
222	  if (input.directSpotsPerComp !== undefined)
223	    patch.directSpotsPerComp = input.directSpotsPerComp
224	  if (input.globalSpots !== undefined) patch.globalSpots = input.globalSpots
225	  if (input.divisionMappings !== undefined) {
226	    patch.divisionMappings = serializeDivisionMappings(input.divisionMappings)
227	  }
228	  if (input.sortOrder !== undefined) patch.sortOrder = input.sortOrder
229	  if (input.notes !== undefined) patch.notes = input.notes
230	
231	  await db
232	    .update(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
233	    .set(patch)
234	    .where(eq(competitionInviteSourcesTable.id, input.id))
235	
236	  const row = await getSourceById(input.id)
237	  if (!row) {
238	    throw new Error("Source was updated but could not be re-read")
239	  }
240	  return row
241	}
242	
243	export async function deleteSource(input: {
244	  id: string
245	  championshipCompetitionId: string
246	}): Promise<boolean> {
247	  const db = getDb()
248	  // Cascade delete the source's per-division allocation rows in the same
249	  // transaction (no FKs per PlanetScale convention — the cascade lives
250	  // here). Tx is the same primitive division capacity uses; PlanetScale
251	  // supports it.
252	  return await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
253	    // Verify the source belongs to the named championship before any
254	    // delete fires. Otherwise a caller authorized for championship A
255	    // could pass a sourceId from championship B and wipe its
256	    // allocation rows cross-tenant — the source-row delete below is
257	    // championship-scoped, but the allocations delete is keyed only by
258	    // sourceId.
259	    const [existing] = await tx
260	      .select({ id: competitionInviteSourcesTable.id })
261	      .from(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
262	      .where(
263	        and(
264	          eq(competitionInviteSourcesTable.id, input.id),
265	          eq(
266	            competitionInviteSourcesTable.championshipCompetitionId,
267	            input.championshipCompetitionId,
268	          ),
269	        ),
270	      )
271	      .limit(1)
272	    if (!existing) return false
273	
274	    await tx
275	      .delete(competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable)
276	      .where(
277	        eq(competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable.sourceId, input.id),
278	      )
279	    await tx
280	      .delete(competitionInviteSourcesTable)
281	      .where(eq(competitionInviteSourcesTable.id, input.id))
282	    return true
283	  })
284	}
285

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Competition Invites Schema
3	 *
4	 * Tables powering ADR-0011 "Competition Invites" — the organizer-facing
5	 * qualification-source → roster → invite-round system.
6	 *
7	 * Phase 1 shipped `competition_invite_sources`. Phase 2 adds
8	 * `competition_invites` — the per-athlete invite row carrying the
9	 * email-locked claim token, status, and origin attribution. Rounds
10	 * (Phase 3) and email templates (Phase 4) follow.
11	 */
12	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Sources schema]]
13	
14	import type { InferSelectModel } from "drizzle-orm"
15	import {
16	  datetime,
17	  index,
18	  int,
19	  mysqlTable,
20	  text,
21	  uniqueIndex,
22	  varchar,
23	} from "drizzle-orm/mysql-core"
24	import {
25	  commonColumns,
26	  createCompetitionInviteId,
27	  createCompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationId,
28	  createCompetitionInviteSourceId,
29	} from "./common"
30	
31	// ============================================================================
32	// Source Kinds
33	// ============================================================================
34	
35	export const COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND = {
36	  COMPETITION: "competition",
37	  SERIES: "series",
38	} as const
39	
40	export type CompetitionInviteSourceKind =
41	  (typeof COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND)[keyof typeof COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND]
42	
43	// ============================================================================
44	// Competition Invite Sources
45	// ============================================================================
46	
47	/**
48	 * A qualification source attached to a championship competition. Exactly one
49	 * of `sourceCompetitionId` / `sourceGroupId` is non-null — the `kind`
50	 * discriminator indicates which. Spot allocations describe how many invitees
51	 * this source contributes to the championship.
52	 *
53	 * Write-time constraints are enforced in helpers, not the DB, to match
54	 * PlanetScale conventions (no FKs).
55	 */
56	export const competitionInviteSourcesTable = mysqlTable(
57	  "competition_invite_sources",
58	  {
59	    ...commonColumns,
60	    id: varchar({ length: 255 })
61	      .primaryKey()
62	      .$defaultFn(() => createCompetitionInviteSourceId())
63	      .notNull(),
64	    // The competition receiving invites from this source.
65	    championshipCompetitionId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
66	    // "competition" | "series"
67	    kind: varchar({ length: 20 })
68	      .$type<CompetitionInviteSourceKind>()
69	      .notNull(),
70	    // When kind = "competition".
71	    sourceCompetitionId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
72	    // When kind = "series". Logical reference to competitionGroupsTable.id.
73	    sourceGroupId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
74	    // For series: how many top-N per comp in the series get a direct slot.
75	    directSpotsPerComp: int(),
76	    // For series: how many additional spots come from the global leaderboard.
77	    // For single-comp: the total top-N that qualifies.
78	    globalSpots: int(),
79	    // JSON: [{ sourceDivisionId, championshipDivisionId, spots? }]
80	    divisionMappings: text(),
81	    // Display order of sources in the organizer sources tab.
82	    sortOrder: int().default(0).notNull(),
83	    // Organizer-visible free-text note about this source.
84	    notes: text(),
85	  },
86	  (table) => [
87	    index("competition_invite_sources_championship_sort_idx").on(
88	      table.championshipCompetitionId,
89	      table.sortOrder,
90	    ),
91	    index("competition_invite_sources_source_competition_idx").on(
92	      table.sourceCompetitionId,
93	    ),
94	    index("competition_invite_sources_source_group_idx").on(
95	      table.sourceGroupId,
96	    ),
97	  ],
98	)
99	
100	export type CompetitionInviteSource = InferSelectModel<

1	/**
2	 * Championship Roster Computation
3	 *
4	 * `getChampionshipRoster` returns the union of every (sourceComp ×
5	 * sourceDivision) leaderboard for the championship's configured sources,
6	 * with each row tagged by the originating competition + division and the
7	 * athlete's email hydrated. The roster page renders this as a single
8	 * table with Competition + Division columns; client-side filters narrow
9	 * the view without round-tripping.
10	 *
11	 * Fan-out: a championship with K sources, each resolving to N
12	 * competitions × M divisions per competition, costs roughly K·N·M
13	 * leaderboard reads, fired in parallel. MVP organizers have 1–3 sources
14	 * with similarly small N/M, so the worst case is bounded under typical
15	 * usage. Leaderboards are cached on the public-facing render path.
16	 *
17	 * ADR-0012 Phase 6: per-(source, championshipDivision) cutoffs are
18	 * applied after leaderboards are fetched. The resolved allocation map
19	 * (`resolveSourceAllocations`) supplies the truncation N for each
20	 * `(sourceId, championshipDivisionId)`. Source-side `globalSpots` /
21	 * `directSpotsPerComp` act as defaults when no override row exists for
22	 * the championship division. See ADR-0012 "Allocation Resolution".
23	 */
24	// @lat: [[competition-invites#Roster computation]]
25	
26	import { inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
27	import { getDb } from "@/db"
28	import {
29	  COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND,
30	  type CompetitionInviteSource,
31	  type CompetitionInviteSourceKind,
32	} from "@/db/schemas/competition-invites"
33	import { competitionsTable } from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
34	import { scalingLevelsTable } from "@/db/schemas/scaling"
35	import { parseCompetitionSettings } from "@/server-fns/competition-divisions-fns"
36	import type { ResolvedSourceAllocations } from "./allocations"
37	import { getCandidatesForSourceComp } from "./candidates"
38	import { type DivisionMapping, listSourcesForChampionship } from "./sources"
39	
40	// ============================================================================
41	// Types
42	// ============================================================================
43	
44	export interface RosterRow {
45	  /** 1-based rank within the source competition's division leaderboard. */
46	  sourcePlacement: number | null
47	  /** Source id that produced this row. */
48	  sourceId: string
49	  /** Source kind snapshot for UI tagging. */
50	  sourceKind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
51	  /** Comp the athlete placed at. */
52	  sourceCompetitionId: string
53	  /** Display name of the source competition. */
54	  sourceCompetitionName: string
55	  /** Division id on the source competition. */
56	  sourceDivisionId: string
57	  /** Display label of the source division (e.g. "RX Men"). */
58	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
59	  /** Stable athlete identity. `userId` may be null if the leaderboard
60	   *  surfaced no user id (rare); `athleteName` is always present. */
61	  userId: string | null
62	  athleteName: string
63	  /** Email address for the athlete, resolved from `userTable` by `userId`.
64	   *  Null when the leaderboard surfaced no user id (rare) or the user
65	   *  has no email on file. */
66	  athleteEmail: string | null
67	  /** Invite-state columns — always null at this layer; the route attaches
68	   *  invite status via `listActiveInvitesFn` keyed by email. */
69	  inviteId: null
70	  inviteStatus: null
71	  roundId: null
72	  roundNumber: null
73	}
74	
75	export interface GetChampionshipRosterInput {
76	  championshipId: string
77	}
78	
79	// ============================================================================
80	// Internal helpers
81	// ============================================================================
82	
83	interface SourceCompetitionRef {
84	  sourceId: string
85	  sourceKind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
86	  competitionId: string
87	  competitionName: string
88	}
89	
90	interface DivisionRef {
91	  competitionId: string
92	  competitionName: string
93	  divisionId: string
94	  divisionLabel: string
95	  sourceId: string
96	  sourceKind: CompetitionInviteSourceKind
97	}
98	
99	interface ChampionshipDivisionInfo {
100	  id: string

300	      scalingGroupId: scalingLevelsTable.scalingGroupId,
301	      position: scalingLevelsTable.position,
302	    })
303	    .from(scalingLevelsTable)
304	    .where(inArray(scalingLevelsTable.scalingGroupId, allGroupIds))
305	
306	  const levelsByGroup = new Map<
307	    string,
308	    Array<{ id: string; label: string; position: number }>
309	  >()
310	  for (const lvl of levels) {
311	    const arr = levelsByGroup.get(lvl.scalingGroupId) ?? []
312	    arr.push({ id: lvl.id, label: lvl.label, position: lvl.position })
313	    levelsByGroup.set(lvl.scalingGroupId, arr)
314	  }
315	  for (const arr of levelsByGroup.values()) {
316	    arr.sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
317	  }
318	
319	  const out: DivisionRef[] = []
320	  for (const comp of comps) {
321	    const sgid = scalingGroupByComp.get(comp.competitionId)
322	    if (!sgid) continue
323	    const compLevels = levelsByGroup.get(sgid) ?? []
324	    for (const lvl of compLevels) {
325	      out.push({
326	        competitionId: comp.competitionId,
327	        competitionName: comp.competitionName,
328	        divisionId: lvl.id,
329	        divisionLabel: lvl.label,
330	        sourceId: comp.sourceId,
331	        sourceKind: comp.sourceKind,
332	      })
333	    }
334	  }
335	  return out
336	}
337	
338	/**
339	 * Compute the cutoff (max number of qualifying placements) for a
340	 * leaderboard fetched for `(source, sourceCompetitionId, sourceDivisionId)`.
341	 *
342	 * Single-comp source: the cutoff is the resolved per-(source,
343	 * championshipDivision) value. Override present → uses override; absent
344	 * → uses the source's `globalSpots` default (applied per division).
345	 *
346	 * Series source: today the leaderboard fan-out yields one entry per
347	 * (seriesComp × division). The per-comp "direct" line uses
348	 * `directSpotsPerComp` as the default; when an override exists for the
349	 * championship division the override is the *total* number of direct
350	 * qualifiers for that division across the *whole series* — not per
351	 * comp. We can't faithfully redistribute that across comps without
352	 * series-leaderboard context (which row from comp A vs comp B counts
353	 * toward the global pool), so series with overrides keeps the current
354	 * full-leaderboard behavior and lets downstream UI / the
355	 * `divisionAllocationTotals` denominator surface the discrepancy.
356	 *
357	 * TODO(ADR-0012 Phase 6): split direct vs. global tiers cleanly for
358	 * series sources with division-level overrides; today the override is
359	 * honored on the per-division *denominator* (Sent tab) but not on the
360	 * per-comp leaderboard truncation here. See ADR-0012 "Allocation
361	 * Resolution" — the global block is still the safe fallback because it
362	 * never *adds* invalid qualifying rows; it can only over-include rows
363	 * the organizer must filter manually.
364	 *
365	 * Returns `null` when no cutoff applies (i.e. include every row).
366	 */
367	function computeCutoffForLeaderboard(args: {
368	  source: CompetitionInviteSource
369	  sourceDivisionId: string
370	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
371	  championshipDivisions: ReadonlyArray<ChampionshipDivisionInfo>
372	  resolved: ResolvedSourceAllocations
373	  divisionMappings: DivisionMapping[]
374	}): number | null {
375	  if (args.source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) {
376	    // Series fallback: keep all rows. Per-comp direct truncation is a
377	    // future improvement (TODO above) — until the (direct, global)
378	    // tiers are split, returning the full leaderboard is the safer
379	    // path because it can only over-include candidate rows the
380	    // organizer manually filters, never silently drop a qualifier.
381	    return null
382	  }
383	
384	  // Single-comp source: cutoff is the resolved spots for the mapped
385	  // championship division. If we can't infer the championship division,
386	  // fall back to no truncation so we don't accidentally drop qualifying
387	  // athletes — the downstream UI still surfaces the correct denominator.
388	  const championshipDivisionId = mapSourceDivisionToChampionship({
389	    sourceDivisionId: args.sourceDivisionId,
390	    sourceDivisionLabel: args.sourceDivisionLabel,
391	    divisionMappings: args.divisionMappings,
392	    championshipDivisions: args.championshipDivisions,
393	  })
394	  if (championshipDivisionId === null) return null
395	  const spots = args.resolved.byDivision[championshipDivisionId]
396	  if (spots === undefined) return null
397	  return spots
398	}
399	
400	// ============================================================================
401	// Main entry
402	// ============================================================================
403	
404	export async function getChampionshipRoster(
405	  input: GetChampionshipRosterInput,
406	): Promise<{ rows: RosterRow[] }> {
407	  const { refs: comps } = await resolveSourceCompetitions(input.championshipId)
408	  if (comps.length === 0) return { rows: [] }
409	
410	  const divisionRefs = await resolveDivisionRefs(comps)
411	  if (divisionRefs.length === 0) return { rows: [] }
412	
413	  // Fan out per (sourceComp × division) candidate fetches in parallel.
414	  // `getCandidatesForSourceComp` is purpose-built for this surface: it
415	  // returns active registrations directly, with no heat / event-status /
416	  // publication gating. The roster used to call `getCompetitionLeaderboard`
417	  // here, which silently dropped divisions whose athletes hadn't been
418	  // heat-assigned — see docs/bugs/0001-invite-candidates-missing-divisions.md.
419	  //

When supplied, each card's "qualifying 31 * spots" line uses the authoritative resolved total instead of the 32 * source-level scalar math. Optional so older callers (and tests) 33 * keep working with the local fallback. */ 34 allocationsBySourceByDivision?: Record> 35 /** Championship divisions in display order — used to render the 36 * per-division preview row under each source card. Optional; the 37 * preview is hidden when omitted. */ 38 championshipDivisions?: ReadonlyArray<{ id: string; label: string }> 39 onEdit?: (source: CompetitionInviteSource) => void 40 onDelete?: (source: CompetitionInviteSource) => void 41 onAdd?: () => void 42 /** Optional render-prop for extra content inside a source card (used to 43 * drop in the series per-comp + global sub-tabs). */ 44 renderSourceExtras?: (source: CompetitionInviteSource) => React.ReactNode 45 } 46 47 function allocatedSpotsFor( 48 source: CompetitionInviteSource, 49 seriesCompCountsById: Record, 50 ): number { 51 if (source.kind === "series") { 52 const compCount = source.sourceGroupId 53 ? (seriesCompCountsById[source.sourceGroupId] ?? 0) 54 : 0 55 return ( 56 (source.directSpotsPerComp ?? 0) * compCount + (source.globalSpots ?? 0) 57 ) 58 } 59 return source.globalSpots ?? 0 60 } 61 62 export function InviteSourcesList({ 63 sources, 64 competitionNamesById, 65 seriesNamesById, 66 seriesCompCountsById = {}, 67 allocationsBySourceByDivision, 68 championshipDivisions, 69 onEdit, 70 onDelete, 71 onAdd, 72 renderSourceExtras, 73 }: InviteSourcesListProps) { 74 // Authoritative resolved total per source. content.content:
370	  sourceDivisionLabel: string
371	  championshipDivisions: ReadonlyArray<ChampionshipDivisionInfo>
372	  resolved: ResolvedSourceAllocations
373	  divisionMappings: DivisionMapping[]
374	}): number | null {
375	  if (args.source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) {
376	    // Series fallback: keep all rows. Per-comp direct truncation is a
377	    // future improvement (TODO above) — until the (direct, global)
378	    // tiers are split, returning the full leaderboard is the safer
379	    // path because it can only over-include candidate rows the
380	    // organizer manually filters, never silently drop a qualifier.
381	    return null
382	  }
383	
384	  // Single-comp source: cutoff is the resolved spots for the mapped
385	  // championship division. If we can't infer the championship division,
386	  // fall back to no truncation so we don't accidentally drop qualifying
387	  // athletes — the downstream UI still surfaces the correct denominator.
388	  const championshipDivisionId = mapSourceDivisionToChampionship({
389	    sourceDivisionId: args.sourceDivisionId,
390	    sourceDivisionLabel: args.sourceDivisionLabel,
391	    divisionMappings: args.divisionMappings,
392	    championshipDivisions: args.championshipDivisions,
393	  })
394	  if (championshipDivisionId === null) return null
395	  const spots = args.resolved.byDivision[championshipDivisionId]
396	  if (spots === undefined) return null
397	  return spots
398	}
399	
400	// ============================================================================
401	// Main entry
402	// ============================================================================
403	
404	export async function getChampionshipRoster(
405	  input: GetChampionshipRosterInput,
406	): Promise<{ rows: RosterRow[] }> {
407	  const { refs: comps } = await resolveSourceCompetitions(input.championshipId)
408	  if (comps.length === 0) return { rows: [] }
409	
410	  const divisionRefs = await resolveDivisionRefs(comps)
411	  if (divisionRefs.length === 0) return { rows: [] }
412	
413	  // Fan out per (sourceComp × division) candidate fetches in parallel.
414	  // `getCandidatesForSourceComp` is purpose-built for this surface: it
415	  // returns active registrations directly, with no heat / event-status /
416	  // publication gating. The roster used to call `getCompetitionLeaderboard`
417	  // here, which silently dropped divisions whose athletes hadn't been
418	  // heat-assigned — see docs/bugs/0001-invite-candidates-missing-divisions.md.
419	  //
420	  // Cutoff is allocation budget metadata, not a candidate filter. The
421	  // organizer needs every eligible athlete on the page so they can pick
422	  // whom to invite — `entries.slice(0, cutoff)` previously dropped
423	  // eligible candidates whenever the source had more registrants than
424	  // the championship-division allocation. The denominator math lives in
425	  // `divisionAllocationTotals` outside this fan-out.
426	  const candidates = await Promise.all(
427	    divisionRefs.map((ref) =>
428	      getCandidatesForSourceComp({
429	        competitionId: ref.competitionId,
430	        divisionId: ref.divisionId,
431	      }).then((c) => ({ ref, entries: c.entries })),
432	    ),
433	  )
434	
435	  const rows: RosterRow[] = []
436	  for (const { ref, entries } of candidates) {
437	    entries.forEach((e, idx) => {
438	      rows.push({
439	        sourcePlacement: idx + 1,
440	        sourceId: ref.sourceId,
441	        sourceKind: ref.sourceKind,
442	        sourceCompetitionId: ref.competitionId,
443	        sourceCompetitionName: ref.competitionName,
444	        sourceDivisionId: ref.divisionId,
445	        sourceDivisionLabel: ref.divisionLabel,
446	        userId: e.userId,
447	        athleteName: e.athleteName,
448	        athleteEmail: e.athleteEmail,
449	        inviteId: null,
450	        inviteStatus: null,
451	        roundId: null,
452	        roundNumber: null,
453	      })
454	    })
455	  }
456	
457	  return { rows }
458	}

 * from the default for this source." A `spots` of `0` is meaningful — it
 * pins the division to zero from this source. Absence of the row means
 * "use the source default."

 * Cascading delete on the source row is handled in

180	// Workers (TZ=UTC) used to render "one day later" for any organizer

482	        serverFn: "updateInviteSourceFn",
483	        attributes: { sourceId: data.id },
484	      },
485	      async () => {
486	        // Resolve the existing row to validate cross-permission against the
487	        // *new* reference target if the caller is changing it.
488	        const existing = await getSourceById(data.id)
489	        if (!existing) throw new Error("Source not found")
490	        if (
