content.content:
[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Claim resolution]] (../../lat.md/competition-invites.md:143-159)

> ## Claim resolution
>
> Read-side helpers for the `/compete/$slug/claim/$token` route live in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts]] (DB reads) and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts]] (pure helpers route files can import without dragging `getDb` into the client bundle).
>
> The split exists because Vite chokes on `cloudflare:workers` when bundling client code, and `getDb` transitively imports it. Route files import only from `identity.ts`; server-side callers can use either module.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts#resolveInviteByToken]] looks up the row directly via `eq(competitionInvitesTable.claimToken, token)` — same pattern as `[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/invite-fns.ts]]` for team invites. It returns `null` on miss so the route can render the generic "invalid link" page without leaking whether the token ever existed. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#assertInviteClaimable]] throws [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#InviteNotClaimableError]] when status, `claimToken`, `expiresAt`, or `activeMarker` fail the live checks — each terminal state maps to a distinct `reason` (`expired`, `declined`, `revoked`, `already_paid`, `not_found`) so the route can render tailored copy.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#identityMatch]] is the pure email-lock gate. Given `{ email }` from the session (or `null`) plus the invite plus an `accountExistsForInviteEmail` boolean (resolved by the caller against `userTable`), it returns the discriminated result the route loader branches on: `{ ok: true }`, `{ ok: false, reason: "wrong_account" | "needs_sign_in" | "needs_sign_up" }`. Case-insensitive on both sides so `Mike@Example.com` and `mike@example.com` compare equal.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#getInviteByTokenFn]] also cross-checks `competition_registrations` when the visitor is signed in as the invited identity: a non-removed row for `(eventId, userId, divisionId)` short-circuits to `not_claimable` with reason `already_paid`. This is the correct defense because it works regardless of which lane (public, organizer-manual, or prior invite claim) created the registration — a still-`pending` invite for an already-registered athlete should not re-enter the payment flow. **This cross-check only fires when the token still resolves**: it sits inside `getInviteByTokenFn` after `resolveInviteByToken`, so it covers stale-but-still-`pending` invites, not paid-then-replayed links.
>
> For the replay-after-paid case, the Stripe workflow nulls `claimToken` on `pending → accepted_paid` (so the same email link can't be used to mint a second registration). `resolveInviteByToken` then returns `null` and `getInviteByTokenFn` returns `not_found`. The route loader at [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/claim/$token.tsx]] handles this by reading the parent route's `userRegistrations` loader data when `reason === "not_found"` and a session exists: a matching active registration triggers the friendly `already_claimed` branch instead of the generic invalid-link error. This keeps the server fn's response surface narrow while letting the route own the "you already registered" recovery UX.
>
> The `already_paid` reason is treated as a soft outcome by the route, not an error: the loader `redirect`s to `/compete/$slug/registered` instead of rendering a destructive alert. Already-registered athletes aren't in an error state — they just landed on the wrong page.
>
>

## This section references:

* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts#resolveInviteByToken]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts:78)
  | export async function resolveInviteByToken(
  |   tokenPlaintext: string,
  | ): Promise<CompetitionInvite | null> {
  |   if (!tokenPlaintext) return null
  |   const db = getDb()
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#assertInviteClaimable]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts:88)
  | export function assertInviteClaimable(
  |   invite: CompetitionInvite,
  |   now: Date = new Date(),
  | ): void {

[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Claim resolution]] (../../lat.md/competition-invites.md:143-159)

> ## Claim resolution
>
> Read-side helpers for the `/compete/$slug/claim/$token` route live in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts]] (DB reads) and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts]] (pure helpers route files can import without dragging `getDb` into the client bundle).
>
> The split exists because Vite chokes on `cloudflare:workers` when bundling client code, and `getDb` transitively imports it. Route files import only from `identity.ts`; server-side callers can use either module.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts#resolveInviteByToken]] looks up the row directly via `eq(competitionInvitesTable.claimToken, token)` — same pattern as `[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/invite-fns.ts]]` for team invites. It returns `null` on miss so the route can render the generic "invalid link" page without leaking whether the token ever existed. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#assertInviteClaimable]] throws [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#InviteNotClaimableError]] when status, `claimToken`, `expiresAt`, or `activeMarker` fail the live checks — each terminal state maps to a distinct `reason` (`expired`, `declined`, `revoked`, `already_paid`, `not_found`) so the route can render tailored copy.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/identity.ts#identityMatch]] is the pure email-lock gate. Given `{ email }` from the session (or `null`) plus the invite plus an `accountExistsForInviteEmail` boolean (resolved by the caller against `userTable`), it returns the discriminated result the route loader branches on: `{ ok: true }`, `{ ok: false, reason: "wrong_account" | "needs_sign_in" | "needs_sign_up" }`. Case-insensitive on both sides so `Mike@Example.com` and `mike@example.com` compare equal.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#getInviteByTokenFn]] also cross-checks `competition_registrations` when the visitor is signed in as the invited identity: a non-removed row for `(eventId, userId, divisionId)` short-circuits to `not_claimable` with reason `already_paid`. This is the correct defense because it works regardless of which lane (public, organizer-manual, or prior invite claim) created the registration — a still-`pending` invite for an already-registered athlete should not re-enter the payment flow. **This cross-check only fires when the token still resolves**: it sits inside `getInviteByTokenFn` after `resolveInviteByToken`, so it covers stale-but-still-`pending` invites, not paid-then-replayed links.
>
> For the replay-after-paid case, the Stripe workflow nulls `claimToken` on `pending → accepted_paid` (so the same email link can't be used to mint a second registration). `resolveInviteByToken` then returns `null` and `getInviteByTokenFn` returns `not_found`. The route loader at [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/claim/$token.tsx]] handles this by reading the parent route's `userRegistrations` loader data when `reason === "not_found"` and a session exists: a matching active registration triggers the friendly `already_claimed` branch instead of the generic invalid-link error. This keeps the server fn's response surface narrow while letting the route own the "you already registered" recovery UX.
>
> The `already_paid` reason is treated as a soft outcome by the route, not an error: the loader `redirect`s to `/compete/$slug/registered` instead of rendering a destructive alert. Already-registered athletes aren't in an error state — they just landed on the wrong page.
>
>

content.content:
[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Seed data]] (../../lat.md/competition-invites.md:280-290)

> ## Seed data
>
> Running `pnpm db:seed` populates `competition_invite_sources` with a ready-to-demo scenario so the organizer `/invites` route renders non-empty. Phase 1 has no source-creation UI, so seeding is the only path to exercise the live tabs.
>
> Scenario defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/scripts/seed/seeders/20-competition-invites.ts]]: a championship competition "2026 WODsmith Invitational" (`comp_inv_championship`) receiving invites from three sources — a Regional Qualifier (`comp_inv_qualifier`) with 5 scored Men's RX athletes + allocation 3 that triggers the roster cutoff divider, Boise Throwdown (`comp_mwfc_a`) reusing MWFC series fixtures, and the MWFC series itself to demo the Series card + sub-tabs. The seeder sits after `19-broadcasts` and its tables are cleaned ahead of competitions in [[apps/wodsmith-start/scripts/seed/cleanup.ts]] (`competition_invites`, `competition_invite_source_division_allocations`, then `competition_invite_sources`).
>
> ADR-0012 added per-(source, championship-division) allocation rows in `competition_invite_source_division_allocations`. The seeder writes them after the sources insert: the Qualifier and Boise sources each carry an explicit override for every championship division (mirroring the per-division `spots` that previously lived only inside the `division_mappings` JSON), and the MWFC series source carries a single override on Men's RX (`spots=4`, vs. the source default of `directSpotsPerComp(1) * compCount(1) + globalSpots(5) = 6`) — the other series divisions intentionally have no row so the source details page demos a mix of "Use default" and explicit-override toggles. Absence of a row signals "use the source default"; never seed `spots: 0` to mean "no override" — that's a hard cap of zero, not a default.
>
> The championship and qualifier each carry two team-of-2 divisions in addition to the individual ones (`slvl_inv_champ_team_rx` / `..._team_sc` and the qualifier-side equivalents). The qualifier is seeded with three Team RX pairs (Thunder, Storm, Havoc) and two Team Scaled pairs (Grit, Pulse) — each as its own `competition_team` with owner=captain + member=teammate, both registered against the team division but only the captain holds a score. The qualifier source's `divisionMappings` covers both indy and team divisions (Team RX `spots=2`, Team Scaled `spots=1`), so the team-roster sub-views also show a cutoff divider. This setup feeds the captain-claim → team-registration form path: when the captain clicks their invite token, the championship registration form opens on a `team_size=2` division and collects the teammate.
>
> The same seeder adds eleven Phase-2 `competition_invites` rows so every shape is inspectable in Drizzle Studio without manual editing. Rows 1–6 cover the individual divisions: pending source invite (Mike), `accepted_paid` (Ryan, activeMarker still "active", `claimToken` nulled), `expired` (Alex, activeMarker NULL), `declined` (Sarah), draft bespoke ("active but `claimToken = NULL`"), and sent bespoke (sponsored athlete). Rows 7–11 mirror those shapes against the team divisions: pending Team RX captain (Tyler), `accepted_paid` Team RX captain (Derek), pending Team Scaled captain (Ashley), `declined` Team Scaled captain (Stephanie), and a sent bespoke Team RX captain ("Sponsored team"). Live `claimToken`s are issued on the rows that need click-through testing — Mike (`seed-invite-mike-pending-men-rx-phase2`), the indy bespoke (`seed-invite-sponsor-bespoke-women-rx-phase2`), Tyler (`seed-invite-tyler-pending-team-rx-phase2`), Ashley (`seed-invite-ashley-pending-team-sc-phase2`), and the team bespoke (`seed-invite-sponsor-bespoke-team-rx-phase2`); all other rows leave `claimToken` NULL.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(invites): collapse allocation default to globalSpots; gate /register submit

The allocation resolver was multiplying directSpotsPerComp * seriesCompCount
+ globalSpots for series sources, applied per division. That diverges from
the product intent: a source's default is just a single per-division number
("top 1 per division qualifies") that overrides per-(source, division) can
replace. The series math meant a source with globalSpots=1 silently
allocated 5 per division (2 direct * 2 comps + 1) when the organizer
expected 1 — extra invites passed the cap because the cap was inflated.

Collapse sourceDefaultPerDivision to source.globalSpots ?? 0 for both kinds
and drop directSpotsPerComp from the schema, server fns, helpers, UI, tests,
and seed data.

The register-submit path also bypassed the soft allocation gate. Mirror the
same assertInviteWithinAllocation + getOccupiedCountForBucket pair from the
claim landing into initiateRegistrationPaymentFn so direct submits to
/register?invite=… can't reach Stripe when the bucket is full.

- pnpm db:push must run after merge to drop direct_spots_per_comp; capture
  with pnpm db:generate before merging to main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --title "fix(invites): collapse allocation default to globalSpots + gate register submit" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Allocation resolver no longer multiplies `directSpotsPerComp * compCount + globalSpots` for series sources. Each source has a single `globalSpots` per-division default that an override row can replace. No multiplication, no series math.
- `directSpotsPerComp` removed from schema, server fns, sources helpers, organizer UI (form, dialog, sources list, source-detail page, series sub-tabs), seeder, and tests.
- `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` now runs the same per-(source, division) allocation guardrail as the claim landing — closes the `/register?invite=…` direct-submit path that previously let extra invites reach Stripe.

## Why
The reported bug was that extras still claimed Stripe spots even though the bucket was supposedly full. The invite in question (token `18-gP4SQk…` on demo) routed to a series source with `direct_spots_per_comp=2, global_spots=1, 2 comps in series`. Old math made the per-division default `2*2+1=5`, so 2 accepted + 1 pending was well under the cap. The product spec is actually: default = `globalSpots` per division ("top 1 finisher qualifies"), and an override on Men's RX = 2 means 2. Exactly that, no extras.

The register-submit gate was also missing — even with correct math, an athlete bookmarking `/register?invite=…` could bypass the claim soft gate and only get caught by the Stripe webhook (charge-then-refund UX). The new gate matches the claim landing's pattern exactly.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm test` — 2629 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm type-check` — clean
- [x] `lat check` — clean
- [ ] Run `pnpm db:push` (and `pnpm db:generate --name=drop-direct-spots-per-comp` before merging) to drop the column
- [ ] On demo: confirm the existing invite for `zac+invite2@wodsmith.com` (token `18-gP4SQk…`) now bounces with `over_allocated` once the column drop is applied — bucket has 2 accepted_paid against the new default of 1 spot
- [ ] Confirm hitting `/compete/$slug/register?invite=<over-capped token>&divisionId=<x>` rejects at submit instead of redirecting to Stripe
- [ ] Confirm a fresh invite within the cap still flows through claim → register → Stripe
- [ ] Confirm the source detail page reads `globalSpots` directly as the default; toggling an override + saving still upserts a `competition_invite_source_division_allocations` row

## Notes for reviewers
- `seriesCompCountsById` is still returned by `listInviteSourcesFn` for backward compat but is no longer wired into any UI. Safe to drop in a follow-up.
- `computeCutoffForLeaderboard` for series sources still returns `null` (full leaderboard) — the cap is enforced downstream at claim/webhook time, not at roster fan-out.
- `lat.md/competition-invites.md` updated: Allocations helper, Source server fns, Roster computation, Registration hand-off + Stripe workflow, Seed data sections.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

content.text:
Series sources work the same as single-competition sources after this change — that's the whole point of the simplification.

## How spots resolve for any source

Single rule, regardless of `kind`:

```
spots(source, division) = override_row.spots ?? source.globalSpots ?? 0
```

That's it. No `directSpotsPerComp`, no comp-count multiplier, no global-vs-direct tier.

## What "series" means now

`kind = "series"` only changes **where the candidates come from**, not how many qualify:

- **Single-competition source**: candidates = registrants of one comp.
- **Series source**: candidates = registrants across every comp in the linked group (e.g. all stops of the MWFC series).

Both then funnel into the same per-(source, division) cap via the formula above. So with your demo's series source `cisrc_01KQBQS6MK84X523A95BBT2AS7`, `globalSpots=1`, no override on `slvl_inv_champ_mrx`:
- Default = 1 spot for Men's RX from this series.
- If you want 2 from Men's RX, add an override row: `(source, slvl_inv_champ_mrx, spots=2)`.
- If you want 3 from Women's RX, add another: `(source, slvl_inv_champ_wrx, spots=3)`.

Each division stands alone; the source contributes whatever its row says (or `globalSpots` as default).

## What stayed series-specific

- The roster/Candidates view fans out across every comp in the series so organizers see the full pool. The full pool isn't truncated at the leaderboard step — the cap is enforced at claim and at register-submit time.
- `competition_invite_sources.source_group_id` (vs `source_competition_id`) still discriminates the two kinds for the candidate-fetch path.

## What got removed

- `direct_spots_per_comp` column + form field
- The "top-N from each comp + N global" tier split (`directCount + globalSpots`)
- The series math `direct * compCount + global` per division

If you want the old "top N from each comp" semantics back later, that becomes a separate kind or a separate per-comp field — explicitly modeled instead of inferred from multiplication.

aiTitle: Debug invite allocation override enforcement bug

content.text:
Got it — I oversimplified. Let me re-confirm the model before changing anything because this is the third revision.

## What I currently have on the PR

One knob per source, applied per division:
```
spots(source, div) = override_row.spots ?? source.globalSpots ?? 0
```

For your MWFC series with `globalSpots=1`, this means **1 per division total** across the whole series — wrong per your clarification.

## What you just described

A series source has **two independent buckets** that don't share a budget:

1. **Per-comp direct**: top N from each individual comp's leaderboard, per division. With 5 comps × top 1 = 5 invites per division (1 from each comp).
2. **Series global**: top M from the *aggregate series leaderboard*, per division. With top 5 = 5 more invites per division.

Inviting all 5 per-comp winners doesn't reduce the global bucket. Inviting from global doesn't reduce per-comp.

## Two ways to model that — which do you want?

**Option A: One series source, two cap fields.** Bring back `direct_spots_per_comp`. A series source row stores both `directSpotsPerComp` (per comp × division) and `globalSpots` (per division, global tier only). The per-(source, division) override row needs two columns too (`directSpots` and `globalSpots`) so an organizer can override either bucket independently for Men's RX. Two buckets, two counters at claim time. Single-comp sources keep just `globalSpots`.

**Option B: Two source rows, each with one cap.** "MWFC Series – per-comp top 1" is a series-kind source with `globalSpots=1` whose claim-time semantics are "per-comp" (i.e. cap = `globalSpots × compCount`). "MWFC Series – global top 5" is a separate row with `globalSpots=5` and global semantics. We'd need a `tier: "per_comp" | "global"` discriminator on `competition_invite_sources` to distinguish the two. Override row stays as is — one number replaces the source default for that source's tier.

A is closer to the original schema (re-adds `directSpotsPerComp`). B matches your "global leaderboard is effectively a different source" framing more literally and keeps the override math one-dimensional.

Which way? Once we pick I'll revise the PR — bringing back `directSpotsPerComp` is straightforward but I want to make sure we land it right this time.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Competition Invites
2	
3	ADR-0011 ships a qualification-source → roster → invite-round system for championship competitions. Organizers declare where invitees come from, see a unified roster from those sources, and — later — send email-locked invites in waves.
4	
5	Phase 1 is read-only: declarative sources, a roster table that joins source leaderboards with invite-state columns set to null, and the organizer route shell. Schema lives in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts]]. ADR reference: `docs/adr/0011-competition-invites.md`.
6	
7	Phase 2 (in progress) lays the data + logic groundwork for email-locked single-send invites: per-athlete invite rows in `competition_invites`, plaintext claim tokens (mirroring `team_invitations.token`), server-side issue + reissue helpers, and bespoke staging. Routes, Stripe workflow wiring, email queue, and the organizer UI follow in the remaining sub-arcs.
8	
9	## Sources schema
10	
11	A qualification source attached to a championship competition. Each row references either a single competition or a series (never both); allocation and division-mapping columns describe how many invitees the source contributes.
12	
13	Defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInviteSourcesTable]] with three indexes — `(championshipCompetitionId, sortOrder)` drives the Sources tab list, `(sourceCompetitionId)` lets us find every championship a given source competition feeds, and `(sourceGroupId)` does the same for series. ULID primary keys generated via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteSourceId]]. No FKs (PlanetScale convention).
14	
15	## Sources helpers
16	
17	Server-side CRUD helpers for the sources table live in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/sources.ts]]. `listSourcesForChampionship`, `createSource`, `updateSource`, `deleteSource`, and `getSourceById` compose the CRUD surface.
18	
19	The write-time constraint "exactly one of `sourceCompetitionId` / `sourceGroupId`" is enforced in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/sources.ts#assertSourceReferenceValid]], not at the DB, because PlanetScale has no check constraints and a typed `InviteSourceValidationError` from one boundary is cleaner than mixing DB and validation errors. `updateSource` re-runs the validator against the post-patch shape so a partial update that breaks the rule fails before reaching the DB.
20	
21	ADR-0012: `deleteSource` wraps the source delete and a cascade delete of the source's `competition_invite_source_division_allocations` rows in a single `db.transaction()` — there is no FK in the DB so the cascade is enforced in code.
22	
23	## Allocations schema
24	
25	ADR-0012's per-(source, championship-division) override table. Each row says "this source contributes exactly N qualifiers to this championship division"; absence of a row means "use the source default."
26	
27	Defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationsTable]]. ULID primary keys via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteSourceDivisionAllocationId]] (`cisda_*` prefix). UNIQUE index on `(sourceId, championshipDivisionId)` enforces one allocation per pair so upsert semantics are unambiguous; a secondary index on `championshipDivisionId` powers the Sent-tab denominator query that sums across sources for one division. No FKs (PlanetScale convention) — references resolve to `competition_invite_sources.id` and `competition_divisions.id` of the championship in code.
28	
29	## Allocations helper
30	
31	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts]] hosts the resolution math that turns "raw override rows + source defaults" into a per-(source, championship division) spot count. Pure where possible so callers can unit-test without DB.
32	
33	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts#resolveSourceAllocations]] is the pure function: given a source row, the championship's divisions, and the source's allocation rows, it returns `{ total, byDivision }`. Algorithm: per championship division, use the override `spots` if a row exists; else use the source default — just `source.globalSpots ?? 0`, regardless of `kind`. Same value applies to every division until an override row says otherwise (e.g. globalSpots=1 → "top 1 finisher per division"; an override on Men's RX = 2 → 2 from this source for that division). `0` overrides are honored ("this source gives this division nothing"); they do not collapse to the default. No multiplication, no series math.
34	
35	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts#listAllocationsForChampionship]] is the DB read: joins `competition_invite_source_division_allocations` with `competition_invite_sources` filtered by `championshipCompetitionId`, returning the raw allocation rows for every source attached to that championship in one query. Callers (the route loader, the roster computation, the claim guardrail) feed those rows into `resolveSourceAllocations`.
36	
37	## Source server fns
38	
39	The organizer endpoints live in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts]] — `listInviteSourcesFn`, `createInviteSourceFn`, `updateInviteSourceFn`, `deleteInviteSourceFn` — gated by `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS` on the championship's organizing team.
40	
41	`listInviteSourcesFn` returns the source rows plus resolved display lookups: `competitionNamesById` (for single-comp sources), `seriesNamesById` (for series sources), and `seriesCompCountsById` (number of competitions in each referenced series — kept for UI display, not allocation math). [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/invite-sources-list.tsx]] derives each card's "qualifying spots" total from the resolved per-(source, division) allocation map; the `globalSpots` scalar is the per-division default with no multiplication.
42	
43	When a source references another competition or series, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#requireSourcePermissions]] also requires `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS` on that source's organizing team. Per ADR Open Question 6 (same-org only for MVP) the two organizing teams must currently match; cross-org references throw. The policy lives in the server fn so a future phase can relax it by deleting a single check. Each fn wraps its handler in `withRequestContext` so `logEntityCreated` / `logEntityUpdated` / `logEntityDeleted` calls carry correlation IDs.
44	
45	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/sources.ts#deleteSource]] runs the allocation cascade and source delete inside a single tx, but pre-validates that the source row matches the supplied `championshipCompetitionId` before either delete fires. Without that pre-check a caller authorized for championship A could pass a `sourceId` belonging to championship B and wipe its allocation rows cross-tenant — the source-row delete is championship-scoped, but the allocations delete is keyed only by `sourceId`. If the pre-check fails the tx no-ops silently, matching the prior "delete returned 0 rows" behavior.
46

content.content:
[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Organizer send UX]] (../../lat.md/competition-invites.md:231-248)

> ## Organizer send UX
>
> Phase 2D makes the organizer invite buttons clickable. See [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/index.tsx]] for the shell.
>
> Three dialogs sit in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/add-bespoke-invitee-dialog.tsx]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/bulk-add-invitees-dialog.tsx]], and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]]. Add/Bulk dialogs wrap the bespoke staging server fns; Send wraps `issueInvitesFn`. The route header carries the three buttons (Add invitee / Bulk add / Send invites N); Send is disabled when no recipients are selected. The Send dialog computes its defaults locally from the championship + division props passed by the parent route — subject defaults to `You're invited to {championshipName} - {divisionLabel}` and the body textarea defaults to a generic invitation message — so organizers don't have to retype either on every send. The greeting line (`Hey {athleteName},`) is rendered automatically per-recipient by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/react-email/competition-invites/invite-email.tsx#CompetitionInviteEmail]] above the body, so the textarea holds the message-only portion.
>
> When the candidates table is filtered to a single source division, the parent route resolves the matching championship division by label and passes it as `defaultDivisionId` to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]] so the dialog opens already pointed at the same division — saves the organizer a click and avoids the "filtered Rx Men candidates, dialog defaulted to Scaled" footgun. The match is label-based (case- and whitespace-insensitive) since source-division and championship-division ids live in different scaling groups; if no label matches, the dialog falls back to the first championship division.
>
> The bulk dialog only exposes an "emails — one per line or comma-separated" affordance even though the underlying `parseBespokePaste` helper still understands the richer CSV/TSV row shape. Before sending, it splits the textarea on `[\n,]+` and rejoins with newlines so a single comma-separated line becomes one email per row (otherwise the helper's CSV parser would treat trailing emails as name fields). The primary CTA reads "Invite", closes the dialog on success, and surfaces the created/duplicate/invalid counts via a sonner toast — `toast.warning` when there are duplicates or invalid lines, `toast.success` otherwise.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/championship-roster-table.tsx]] now accepts optional `selectedKeys` / `onToggleSelection` / `onToggleAll` props. When the parent supplies them a checkbox column appears; rows with no `athleteEmail` are disabled with a "No email on file" tooltip so the organizer can't try to invite an athlete without a resolvable address. The table also takes an optional `getInviteStatusForRow(row)` callback that returns the row's active-invite status (`pending`, `accepted_paid`, or `null`). The status pill renders "Registered" for `accepted_paid`, "Invited" for `pending`, and "Not invited" when null — paid invitees are visually distinct from still-pending ones, since `activeMarker = "active"` would otherwise lump both under "Invited". Any non-null status disables the checkbox with an "Already invited" tooltip and excludes the row from select-all so the "Send invites (N)" chip can't silently disagree with the visible checked-box count. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/roster.ts#RosterRow]] now carries `athleteEmail` — hydrated at the end of `getChampionshipRoster` via a single bulk `userTable` lookup so the Send dialog has an email to put on each recipient.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#listActiveInvitesFn]] returns an `ActiveInviteSummary` projection that includes `claimToken` so the organizer UI can render the full claim URL inline (copy-to-clipboard for resend / hand-delivery is the user-visible motivator for plaintext storage). The same payload still carries a `hasClaimToken` boolean so the draft-vs-already-sent distinction is a single field check rather than an emptiness test.
>
> The draft-bespoke section renders inline below the roster table, pulling from `listActiveInvitesFn`. Checked draft rows flow into the recipient list alongside selected source rows; the Send dialog shows a union of both with a recipient-count chip. After a successful send the route invalidates so the just-activated bespoke rows fall off the draft list (they now have tokens).
>
> ADR-0012 Phase 4 surfaces per-(source, championship-division) allocation in the Candidates tab and the Send dialog. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/championship-roster-table.tsx]] accepts `allocationsBySourceByDivision` + `championshipDivisions` and renders a compact allocation strip above the table. The strip leads with a `Qualifies N · M/T sources` summary (total qualifying spots, count of allocated source-division groups vs. total) and one chip per allocated `(sourceId, sourceDivisionLabel)` group present in the visible rows; the `→ {championshipDivisionLabel}` segment is only rendered when source and target labels differ (so same-label cases collapse to just `{sourceDivisionLabel} · Top N`), removing the `"X → X"` redundancy the original banner had. Source-division groups with no matching championship division (or a resolved `0`) are tucked behind a single amber `N unallocated` popover trigger that lists them grouped by source competition — keeping the noisy "No allocation" rows out of the primary scan path while leaving them one click away. Same case-insensitive label match the route uses for the Send dialog's `defaultDivisionId`. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]] now accepts `allocationsBySourceByDivision`, `existingActiveCountsBySourceByDivision`, and `sourceLabelsById`. The dialog buckets the staged recipients by `(sourceId, targetDivisionId)`, renders an inline per-bucket breakdown ("`{sourceLabel}` → `{divisionLabel}`: spot N of M") in the recipients panel, and surfaces a non-blocking amber alert listing every bucket where `existing + incoming > allocation` (Round 1 over-invites are allowed by design; the warning is informational and the Send button stays enabled). Bespoke recipients (`sourceId === null`) are excluded from the bucket math entirely. The route loader computes `existingActiveCountsBySourceByDivision` from the audit projection (`activeMarker === "active"` filtered to source-origin rows) and passes it to the dialog alongside the resolved allocation map and a `sourceLabelsById` derived via `resolveSourceLabel`. Both the table and the dialog short-circuit gracefully when the new props are absent — older callers and the existing test fixtures don't have to change.
>

## This section references:

* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/index.tsx]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/index.tsx)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/add-bespoke-invitee-dialog.tsx]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/add-bespoke-invitee-dialog.tsx)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/bulk-add-invitees-dialog.tsx]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/bulk-add-invitees-dialog.tsx)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/react-email/competition-invites/invite-email.tsx#CompetitionInviteEmail]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/react-email/competition-invites/invite-email.tsx:25)
  | export const CompetitionInviteEmail = ({
  | 	championshipName,
  | 	divisionLabel,
  | 	claimUrl,

toolUseResult.stdout:
[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Organizer send UX]] (../../lat.md/competition-invites.md:231-248)

> ## Organizer send UX
>
> Phase 2D makes the organizer invite buttons clickable. See [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/invites/index.tsx]] for the shell.
>
> Three dialogs sit in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/add-bespoke-invitee-dialog.tsx]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/bulk-add-invitees-dialog.tsx]], and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]]. Add/Bulk dialogs wrap the bespoke staging server fns; Send wraps `issueInvitesFn`. The route header carries the three buttons (Add invitee / Bulk add / Send invites N); Send is disabled when no recipients are selected. The Send dialog computes its defaults locally from the championship + division props passed by the parent route — subject defaults to `You're invited to {championshipName} - {divisionLabel}` and the body textarea defaults to a generic invitation message — so organizers don't have to retype either on every send. The greeting line (`Hey {athleteName},`) is rendered automatically per-recipient by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/react-email/competition-invites/invite-email.tsx#CompetitionInviteEmail]] above the body, so the textarea holds the message-only portion.
>
> When the candidates table is filtered to a single source division, the parent route resolves the matching championship division by label and passes it as `defaultDivisionId` to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]] so the dialog opens already pointed at the same division — saves the organizer a click and avoids the "filtered Rx Men candidates, dialog defaulted to Scaled" footgun. The match is label-based (case- and whitespace-insensitive) since source-division and championship-division ids live in different scaling groups; if no label matches, the dialog falls back to the first championship division.
>
> The bulk dialog only exposes an "emails — one per line or comma-separated" affordance even though the underlying `parseBespokePaste` helper still understands the richer CSV/TSV row shape. Before sending, it splits the textarea on `[\n,]+` and rejoins with newlines so a single comma-separated line becomes one email per row (otherwise the helper's CSV parser would treat trailing emails as name fields). The primary CTA reads "Invite", closes the dialog on success, and surfaces the created/duplicate/invalid counts via a sonner toast — `toast.warning` when there are duplicates or invalid lines, `toast.success` otherwise.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/championship-roster-table.tsx]] now accepts optional `selectedKeys` / `onToggleSelection` / `onToggleAll` props. When the parent supplies them a checkbox column appears; rows with no `athleteEmail` are disabled with a "No email on file" tooltip so the organizer can't try to invite an athlete without a resolvable address. The table also takes an optional `getInviteStatusForRow(row)` callback that returns the row's active-invite status (`pending`, `accepted_paid`, or `null`). The status pill renders "Registered" for `accepted_paid`, "Invited" for `pending`, and "Not invited" when null — paid invitees are visually distinct from still-pending ones, since `activeMarker = "active"` would otherwise lump both under "Invited". Any non-null status disables the checkbox with an "Already invited" tooltip and excludes the row from select-all so the "Send invites (N)" chip can't silently disagree with the visible checked-box count. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/roster.ts#RosterRow]] now carries `athleteEmail` — hydrated at the end of `getChampionshipRoster` via a single bulk `userTable` lookup so the Send dialog has an email to put on each recipient.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#listActiveInvitesFn]] returns an `ActiveInviteSummary` projection that includes `claimToken` so the organizer UI can render the full claim URL inline (copy-to-clipboard for resend / hand-delivery is the user-visible motivator for plaintext storage). The same payload still carries a `hasClaimToken` boolean so the draft-vs-already-sent distinction is a single field check rather than an emptiness test.
>
> The draft-bespoke section renders inline below the roster table, pulling from `listActiveInvitesFn`. Checked draft rows flow into the recipient list alongside selected source rows; the Send dialog shows a union of both with a recipient-count chip. After a successful send the route invalidates so the just-activated bespoke rows fall off the draft list (they now have tokens).
>
> ADR-0012 Phase 4 surfaces per-(source, championship-division) allocation in the Candidates tab and the Send dialog. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/championship-roster-table.tsx]] accepts `allocationsBySourceByDivision` + `championshipDivisions` and renders a compact allocation strip above the table. The strip leads with a `Qualifies N · M/T sources` summary (total qualifying spots, count of allocated source-division groups vs. total) and one chip per allocated `(sourceId, sourceDivisionLabel)` group present in the visible rows; the `→ {championshipDivisionLabel}` segment is only rendered when source and target labels differ (so same-label cases collapse to just `{sourceDivisionLabel} · Top N`), removing the `"X → X"` redundancy the original banner had. Source-division groups with no matching championship division (or a resolved `0`) are tucked behind a single amber `N unallocated` popover trigger that lists them grouped by source competition — keeping the noisy "No allocation" rows out of the primary scan path while leaving them one click away. Same case-insensitive label match the route uses for the Send dialog's `defaultDivisionId`. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]] now accepts `allocationsBySourceByDivision`, `existingActiveCountsBySourceByDivision`, and `sourceLabelsById`. The dialog buckets the staged recipients by `(sourceId, targetDivisionId)`, renders an inline per-bucket breakdown ("`{sourceLabel}` → `{divisionLabel}`: spot N of M") in the recipients panel, and surfaces a non-blocking amber alert listing every bucket where `existing + incoming > allocation` (Round 1 over-invites are allowed by design; the warning is informational and the Send button stays enabled). Bespoke recipients (`sourceId === null`) are excluded from the bucket math entirely. The route loader computes `existingActiveCountsBySourceByDivision` from the audit projection (`activeMarker === "active"` filtered to source-origin rows) and passes it to the dialog alongside the resolved allocation map and a `sourceLabelsById` derived via `resolveSourceLabel`. Both the table and the dialog short-circuit gracefully when the new props are absent — older callers and the existing test fixtures don't have to change.
>

input.new_string:
/**
 * Default allocation for a championship division when no override row
 * exists for `(source.id, division.id)`.
 *
 * - `competition` / `series_global`: source.globalSpots is the per-
 *   division default. Same value across every division until an
 *   override says otherwise.
 * - `series`: 0 — the per-comp grouping kind has no source-level
 *   default. Per-division override is the only knob, so an unconfigured
 *   division contributes nothing.
 *
 * Null `globalSpots` collapses to 0 in either case.
 */
function sourceDefaultPerDivision(source: CompetitionInviteSource): number {
  if (source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) return 0
  return source.globalSpots ?? 0
}

/**
 * Default allocation for a championship division when no override row
 * exists for `(source.id, division.id)`.
 *
 * - `competition` / `series_global`: source.globalSpots is the per-
 *   division default. Same value across every division until an
 *   override says otherwise.
 * - `series`: 0 — the per-comp grouping kind has no source-level
 *   default. Per-division override is the only knob, so an unconfigured
 *   division contributes nothing.
 *
 * Null `globalSpots` collapses to 0 in either case.
 */
function sourceDefaultPerDivision(source: CompetitionInviteSource): number {
  if (source.kind === COMPETITION_INVITE_SOURCE_KIND.SERIES) return 0
  return source.globalSpots ?? 0
}

"use client" /** * Invite Sources List — organizer-visible roster of all qualification * sources feeding the championship, plus an allocated-spots summary card * (matches `project/invites/sources.jsx` modulo visual polish). */ import type React from "react" import { Layers, Trophy } from "lucide-react" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle, } from "@/components/ui/card" import type { CompetitionInviteSource } from "@/db/schemas/competition-invites" interface InviteSourcesListProps { sources: CompetitionInviteSource[] competitionNamesById: Record seriesNamesById: Record /** ADR-0012: resolved per-(source, championshipDivision) allocation * map, summed per source. When supplied, each card's "qualifying * spots" line uses the authoritative resolved total. Optional so * tests can fall back to the source's `globalSpots` scalar. */ allocationsBySourceByDivision?: Record> /** Championship divisions in display order — used to render the * per-division preview row under each source card. input.old_string:
## Sources schema

A qualification source attached to a championship competition. Each row references either a single competition or a series (never both); allocation and division-mapping columns describe how many invitees the source contributes.

Defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInviteSourcesTable]] with three indexes — `(championshipCompetitionId, sortOrder)` drives the Sources tab list, `(sourceCompetitionId)` lets us find every championship a given source competition feeds, and `(sourceGroupId)` does the same for series. ULID primary keys generated via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteSourceId]]. No FKs (PlanetScale convention).
input.new_string:
## Sources schema

A qualification source attached to a championship competition. Each row picks a discriminator via `kind` and references exactly one upstream — a single competition (`kind = "competition"`) or a series (`kind = "series"` for the per-comp grouping, `kind = "series_global"` for the series-aggregate leaderboard). Allocation and division-mapping columns describe how many invitees the source contributes.

The series tier split is intentional: "series" and "series_global" are modeled as separate source rows so each tier owns its own per-(source, division) allocation bucket. Inviting the top-1-per-comp qualifier from a series source doesn't reduce the global cap on a series_global source, and vice versa — the organizer chooses which tier they want by adding one or both as distinct source rows.

Defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInviteSourcesTable]] with three indexes — `(championshipCompetitionId, sortOrder)` drives the Sources tab list, `(sourceCompetitionId)` lets us find every championship a given source competition feeds, and `(sourceGroupId)` does the same for series (covers both series kinds). ULID primary keys generated via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteSourceId]]. No FKs (PlanetScale convention).

input.old_string: [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts#resolveSourceAllocations]] is the pure function: given a source row, the championship's divisions, and the source's allocation rows, it returns `{ total, byDivision }`. Algorithm: per championship division, use the override `spots` if a row exists; else use the source default — just `source.globalSpots ?? 0`, regardless of `kind`. Same value applies to every division until an override row says otherwise (e.g. globalSpots=1 → "top 1 finisher per division"; an override on Men's RX = 2 → 2 from this source for that division). `0` overrides are honored ("this source gives this division nothing"); they do not collapse to the default. No multiplication, no series math.
input.new_string: [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/allocations.ts#resolveSourceAllocations]] is the pure function: given a source row, the championship's divisions, and the source's allocation rows, it returns `{ total, byDivision }`. Algorithm: per championship division, use the override `spots` if a row exists; else use the source default. Default is kind-dependent — `competition` and `series_global` use `source.globalSpots ?? 0`, while `series` returns 0 (the per-comp grouping has no source-level default; per-division override is the only knob). `0` overrides are honored ("this source gives this division nothing"); they do not collapse to the default. No multiplication, no series math: each source row is a single bucket of qualifying spots, and the override is the absolute total for that championship division from that source.

When a source references another competition or series, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#requireSourcePermissions]] also requires `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS` on that source's organizing team. Per ADR Open Question 6 (same-org only for MVP) the two organizing teams must currently match; cross-org references throw. The policy lives in the server fn so a future phase can relax it by deleting a single check. Each fn wraps its handler in `withRequestContext` so `logEntityCreated` / `logEntityUpdated` / `logEntityDeleted` calls carry correlation IDs.

input.new_string:
## Sources schema

A qualification source attached to a championship competition. Three kinds via the `kind` discriminator: `competition`, `series`, and `series_global` — each row references exactly one upstream.

`competition` references a single comp; `series` references a competition group and treats it as a per-comp grouping; `series_global` references the same kind of group but represents the series-aggregate leaderboard tier. Allocation and division-mapping columns describe how many invitees the source contributes.

The series tier split is intentional: "series" and "series_global" are modeled as separate source rows so each tier owns its own per-(source, division) allocation bucket. Inviting the top-1-per-comp qualifier from a series source doesn't reduce the global cap on a series_global source, and vice versa — the organizer chooses which tier they want by adding one or both as distinct source rows.

+`competition` references a single comp; `series` references a competition group and treats it as a per-comp grouping; `series_global` references the same kind of group but represents the series-aggregate leaderboard tier. Allocation and division-mapping columns describe how many invitees the source contributes.

The series tier split is intentional: "series" and "series_global" are modeled as separate source rows so each tier owns its own per-(source, division) allocation bucket. Inviting the top-1-per-comp qualifier from a series source doesn't reduce the global cap on a series_global source, and vice versa — the organizer chooses which tier they want by adding one or both as distinct source rows.

Defined by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInviteSourcesTable]] with three indexes — `(championshipCompetitionId, sortOrder)` drives the Sources tab list, `(sourceCompetitionId)` lets us find every championship a given source competition feeds, and `(sourceGroupId)` does the same for series (covers both series kinds). ULID primary keys generated via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteSourceId]]. No FKs (PlanetScale convention).

content.text:
Pushed to PR #441 (`fix/invite-allocation-flat-default`).

## What's now in the PR

**Three source kinds** in the dropdown when adding/editing a source:
- **Single competition** — pick a comp + globalSpots ("top N qualifies per division").
- **Series** — pick a series. Per-comp grouping. No globalSpots field. Per-division override on the source-detail page is the only knob.
- **Series global leaderboard** — pick a series + globalSpots ("top N from the series global leaderboard per division").

Each is its own row in `competition_invite_sources` and owns its own allocation bucket — claim/register-time counters track each kind independently, so per-comp invites don't reduce the global cap and vice versa.

**Cap math** (`sourceDefaultPerDivision`):
- `competition` / `series_global`: `source.globalSpots ?? 0` per division.
- `series`: 0 per division (override only).
- All three: per-(source, division) override row replaces the default when present.

For your demo data, the existing `cisrc_01KQBQS6MK84X523A95BBT2AS7` series source becomes a "Series" kind row with no global cap. To bring back the global tier, add a new "Series global leaderboard" source on the same series and set its global spots there.

Tests: 2631 pass. Type check + `lat check` green.

Reminder: still need `pnpm db:push` against demo (and `pnpm db:generate` before merge) so the dropped `direct_spots_per_comp` column gets removed in the live schema.
